title: Product accordion tabs render raw HTML markup — richtext schema rejects <ol>/<li>/<div>
description:
On product pages, the How To Use / Description / Benefits / Ingredients accordion shows raw HTML (
  1. Dispense and emulsify ...
    2. ) as plain text. Root cause: sections/product.liquid and sections/section-product.liquid declare raw_content_1 through raw_content_5 as type:richtext in section schema. Shopify's richtext type rejects
      /
        /
      • /
        and HTML-escapes the entire input when those tags are present. Fix: change schema type from "richtext" to "html" for all raw_content_* fields. Caveat: existing escaped content remains escaped — merchant must re-save affected products in theme editor. If many products affected, follow-up with unescape filter chain as separate fix.

title: Missing blog body images — malformed in article.content
description: Several blog posts (e.g. /blogs/hair-education/2026-best-in-beauty-hair-protocols-essentials — 10 of 11 broken) render tags whose parent tag is missing its closing > before the . Browsers refuse to render images inside malformed anchor wraps. Source HTML in article.content is already malformed (rich-text editor or vendor app produced bad HTML). The article-content-with-img-dims.liquid post-processor preserves the malformation. Fix: add defensive replace chain at top of post-processor to repair target="_blank"

title: Liquid syntax error on branded collection pages — quote-style mismatch in title-strip replace chain
description: Live-site Liquid syntax error (Unexpected character é) renders verbatim on /collections/kerastase, /collections/kerastase-genesis, /collections/kerastase-chroma-absolu. Root cause: replace: filter chain in snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid:187 and snippets/product-grid-item.liquid:294 mixes single + double quotes; entries 3 and 4 use single quotes around strings containing apostrophe (L'Oréal) which terminates the Liquid string literal early. Fix: standardize all 14 entries to double quotes. Bug provenance: introduced 2026-04-29 commit 0614791 (ujh) and copy-pasted into branded variant on 2026-05-03 commit cb485b9 (aoe consolidation).

title: [bug] Site-wide CLS 0.42 — main content shifts on initial paint (above-main reveal)
description:
PSI mobile audit 2026-05-18 attributed 0.421 cumulative CLS (single shift event) to `body > main#MainContent` shifting down on initial paint. This is the largest single shift on every page on the site — it dwarfs the Judge.me medals (0.185, fixed in gso0) and LoyaltyLion (0.046) shifts.

## What we know
- Shift target: `main#MainContent` direct child of `<body>`
- Shift magnitude: score 0.421 (very large — implies near-full viewport impact × significant distance)
- Affects: every page (measured on a 404 page where main content is just a 9-line message, so the shift is from chrome above main, not main content itself)
- Static elements above main (measured on hairmnl.com homepage, desktop):
  - `#shopify-section-...__popups` (h=0)
  - `#shopify-section-...__cart` (h=0)
  - `#shopify-section-...__header` (h=70)
  - `#shopify-section-...__announcement` (h=36, .announcement__wrapper)
  - `.skip-link` (h=1)
  - cookie consent windows (display:none)

Total static above-main: 107px. Whatever causes a 0.421 shift must add significantly more height than that, OR shift earlier and farther.

## Hypothesis
1. Announcement bar appears LATE (ticker JS reveals after FCP) — bd hairmnl-theme-7i0 already tracks this. May be the main cause OR may overlap.
2. Header growing — mobile menu trigger, sticky→non-sticky transition, or cart count badge being injected by JS.
3. A `display:none` element above main becoming visible (cookie banner, GDPR consent, NPS prompt).
4. Web-font loading without font-display:swap-with-size-adjust → text reflow that pushes layout.

## Investigation needed
1. Chrome DevTools Performance trace on a real page (homepage or PDP) with mobile throttling.
2. Identify the shift event timestamp and which element's bounding rect changed.
3. Capture the layout-shift entries via PerformanceObserver and inspect source nodes.

## Related
- bd hairmnl-theme-7i0 (P3, in_progress) — announcement bar CLS via ticker init timing. May be the same root cause or a subset.
- bd hairmnl-theme-987 (closed) — Phase 2 CLS work, did not address this specific shift.
- bd hairmnl-theme-gso0 (closed today) — fixed the 0.185 footer shift; this is the remaining big one.

## Expected impact
Fixing this would cut ~0.4 from site-wide mobile CLS — would shift hairmnl.com from POOR to NEEDS-IMPROVEMENT or GOOD on most pages. High priority.
notes: Claiming for diagnosis. Starting with deeper PSI JSON parse to extract previousRect/currentRect for the 0.421 main shift, then Chrome DevTools instrumentation on the live homepage.

title: Search section on homepage — CLS source surfaced after ujg6.5
description:
**Discovered during ujg6.5 verification.** A search results section (type=search, file: sections/search.liquid) is on the homepage at templates/index.json line 145 (block UUID 2070a358-5e4d-4784-8b58-016e8eb567a2). After fixing menu-buttons CLS in ujg6.5, the search section emerged as the new dominant CLS source.

## Measured (post-ujg6.5 desktop home n=2, cache-busted)

- Total CLS: 0.798
- Top 2 shifts both attributed to the same shopify-section wrapper (search section UUID 2070a358)
  - Score 0.519
  - Score 0.279
- Combined: 0.798

## Likely root cause

`sections/search.liquid` line 15 wraps in `<section class="wrapper--narrow pt1 pb1 hidden-pocket" ...>`.

The `.hidden-pocket` rule is defined in `assets/custom-theme.css` line 636-639:
```css
@media screen and (max-width: 1008px) {
  .hidden-pocket {
    display: none !important;
  }
}
```

This is **mobile-only display:none**. On desktop (>1008px) the section is always visible — so `hidden-pocket` shouldn't cause CLS on the desktop measurement. The actual cause may be something else affecting the section's height — possibly `.wrapper--narrow` or `.search-bar` styling differences pre/post custom-theme.css.

## Steps

1. Reproduce: PSI desktop on `/?preview_theme_id=141168312419&_v=test`. Confirm shift attributed to the search section.
2. Identify which custom-theme.css rule is changing the section's height.
3. Either:
   - Inline the critical layout CSS into `sections/search.liquid` (mirroring the ujg6.5 fix for menu-buttons), OR
   - Remove the search section from `templates/index.json` if it's not intentionally on the homepage (audit with operator), OR
   - Wrap the section in a `min-height` reservation if option 1/2 aren't feasible

## Files

- `sections/search.liquid` (add `<style>` block with critical CSS — same pattern as menu-buttons fix)
- OR `templates/index.json` (remove section if not needed)

## Acceptance

- Desktop home CLS contribution from search section UUID 2070a358-... ≤ 0.05
- Total desktop home CLS ≤ 0.10 (combined with ujg6.5 fix; was 0.532 pre-fix, 0.798 post-ujg6.5)
- kt0 lint clean
- Pushed to dev theme 141168312419

## Out of scope

- Other CLS sources (Phase 1.x tickets)
- The search results page itself (`/search`) — that template uses the same section but with `search.performed=true`; CLS behaviour there is different

title: [bug] Blog post CLS 0.60 + LCP 32s — best-hair-color-for-morenas
description:
PSI audit 2026-05-18 found catastrophic CWV failures on /blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas.

## PSI results (n=3, mobile, 2026-05-18)
- Score: 14
- LCP: 31.95s (threshold: ≤2.5s Good) — FAIL
- CLS: 0.600 (threshold: ≤0.10 Good) — FAIL
- TBT: 862ms (threshold: ≤200ms Good) — FAIL
- FCP: 5.03s

## Why this is urgent
- CLS 0.6 is 6x the "Needs Improvement" ceiling (0.10). This is layout-shift visible to every visitor.
- LCP 32s is ~12x the Good threshold. The page's largest content element is loading extremely late.
- Score 14 = bottom 5% of the web.

## Hypothesis
This is a blog article page. Likely culprits:
1. **Large hero/featured image without explicit dimensions** — shifts layout when it loads.
2. **Pro Blogger app** (bd hairmnl-theme-01vl.2) — render-blocking CSS injection. Pro Blogger was deferred for templates but blog articles are a primary vector.
3. **Embedded video or iframe** — late-loading media can cause massive LCP.
4. **No image width/height on article images** — standard blog article CLS cause.
5. **Ad injection or third-party widget** in article body loading async and causing shifts.

## Investigation needed
1. Load the URL in Chrome DevTools → Performance tab → identify LCP element and CLS shift nodes.
2. Check if Pro Blogger is injecting any render-blocking resource on this article template.
3. Check if the blog article template has an og:image / hero image without explicit width/height.
4. Check if any YouTube/Vimeo embed is present in the article body.
notes:
Starting diagnosis via Chrome MCP DevTools Performance trace. Will identify LCP element + CLS shift sources before deciding fix path.
DIAGNOSIS COMPLETE 2026-05-18 (Opus 4.7 / High)

## Finding 1: The URL is a 404 (not a real article)
`/blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas` returns HTTP 404. The "32s LCP / CLS 0.6" PSI measurements are for the 404 PAGE, not a blog article. Body class: `404-not-found`. Operator action: republish article OR add 301 redirect OR remove URL from PSI list. NOT a theme code bug for this specific URL.

## Finding 2: CLS sources on the 404 (also affect every other page)
Lighthouse layout-shifts audit (15 entries, total 0.652):
- 0.421: `body#404-not-found > main#MainContent` — main shifts down. Cause TBD (likely announcement bar or sticky-element reveal above main). Needs deeper investigation. Filing as follow-up.
- 0.185: `#shopify-section-footer` — Judge.me medals widget growing 80px→411px on mobile reveal. ✓ FIXED in this commit.
- 0.046: `#lion-notification-container` — LoyaltyLion notification panel slides in. Already deferred to user-interaction (snippets/loyaltylion.liquid); panel reveal triggers on signed-out users only. Trade-off: keeping for now since LL is a deliberate engagement surface.

## Fix shipped
commit d4b877d (on origin/main, pushed to live 131664707683):
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid: .jdgm-medals-wrapper.jdgm-hidden min-height
- Old: 80px (single rule)
- New: 411px mobile / 134px desktop (matched to measured rendered height)
- Expected CLS reduction: ~0.185 on every page (PSI mobile)

## Verification path
1. Live theme has the new CSS (verified via shopify theme pull). CDN edge cache may take 5-10 min to warm.
2. Re-PSI a real (non-404) page tomorrow once CDN is warm:
   - https://www.hairmnl.com/ (homepage)
   - https://www.hairmnl.com/products/davines-momo-shampoo (PDP)
3. CrUX field data: ~7-14 days to reflect.

## Follow-ups filed
- New bd issue for the 0.421 main-content shift investigation (needs DevTools Performance trace; could overlap with bd hairmnl-theme-7i0 announcement bar work).
- gso0 itself closes here — the theme-code action is done; URL fix is operator content.

title: P5.1 (closeout): epic-audit on p8-perf diff
description:
Per global rule in `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`: every CC-orchestrated epic with executable diff must close with `epic-audit` review.

## Steps

1. Baseline tag: prior audit tag, OR repo first-commit. Use `git rev-parse HEAD` as `$EPIC_HEAD` after Phase 4 lands.
2. Run: `epic-audit p8-perf --path /Users/y9378348c/Projects/hairmnl-theme --baseline $BASELINE --head $EPIC_HEAD`
3. Audit framing for Phase 3 of epic-audit: focus on:
   - Fix regressions (did P1.5's CSS defer break any styling that P1.2's image dimension audit relied on?)
   - Incomplete fixes (did a TAE migration leave a legacy snippet behind?)
   - Interaction effects (did P3.4's lazy-render combine badly with P3.3's content-visibility?)
   - NOT re-litigating original findings
4. Triage Phase 3 findings into < 3 code-changing follow-ups (escape hatch) OR file each as a follow-up bd ticket.

## Acceptance

- Either: epic-audit run, findings recorded, follow-up tickets filed
- Or: closed as `won't-do` with triage notes citing < 3 code-changing follow-ups

## Model

Opus 4.7 / High. Per global rule: closeout is Final Code Review territory; do NOT offload.

title: P4.1: PSI verification matrix — same methodology as P0.1, n=5 each
description:
Goal: re-run the same PSI matrix from P0.1, with the SAME methodology, to verify Phase 1-3 work delivered the acceptance criteria.

## Steps

1. Same 5 templates from P0.1: /, /collections/best-sellers, /products/<top>, /cart, /collections/loreal-professionnel.
2. Mobile + desktop, n=5 (or n≥3 if PSI 500s recur).
3. Three comparison points:
   - P6 live (re-measure — establish that P6 hasn't drifted)
   - Bare P8 (reference from 2026-05-17 baseline; do not re-measure unless feasible)
   - Current P8 (post-Phase-1-2-3 dev theme)
4. Output: appends a "P4.1 verification (2026-05-XX)" section to `os2-migration/perf-baseline-comparison.md` mirroring P0.1's format.

## Acceptance criteria check (epic-level)

Verify each acceptance criterion against the new data:
- [ ] Desktop PSI ≥ 70 on all 5 templates
- [ ] Mobile PSI ≥ 60 on all 5 templates
- [ ] CLS ≤ 0.05 lab on all 5
- [ ] TBT ≤ 500ms desktop / ≤ 800ms mobile on all 5
- [ ] LCP ≤ 2.0s desktop / ≤ 3.5s mobile on home + PDP

## Decision

- If all acceptance criteria pass: epic ready for closeout (P5.1).
- If 1-2 templates fall short: file targeted follow-up tickets, run another iteration.
- If ≥ 3 templates fall short: stop, write a "Phase 1-3 incomplete" memo, re-scope.

## Out of scope

- CrUX field projection (P4.2)
- CI integration (P4.3)

title: P3.1: TAE migration — 10 GREEN apps via Shopify admin toggles
description:
Goal: migrate 10 of the 14 🟢 GREEN-tier apps from `os2-migration/app-compatibility-matrix.md` from theme-side embedded code to Theme App Extensions (TAE). This removes the apps' JS from the main theme bundle and is OS 2.0 feature P6 cannot use.

## GREEN apps to migrate (per matrix)

| # | App | TAE Block | Current Mechanism |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | Smart SEO (core) | smartseo | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 3 | Smart SEO (broken-link) | brokenLinkDetection | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 4 | Klaviyo | klaviyo-onsite-embed | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 7 | Shopify Inbox | chat | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 8 | Nova EU Cookie Bar | app-embed | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 10 | Hextom Timer Bar | ctb-embeded-block | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 11 | LoyaltyLion (SDK) | embed-sdk | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 12 | LoyaltyLion (main) | embed-main | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 13 | LDT Gift Wrap | embed | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 14 | OXI Social Login | social-login-embed | App embed (TAE-ready already) |
| 16 | Searchanise | (toggle) | Confirmed TAE-ready per 2026-05-17 vendor docs |

## Approach

This is **CC-only work** — it's Shopify admin clicking, not code. For each app:

1. Open Shopify admin → Online Store → Themes → Customize (on dev theme 141168312419).
2. Navigate to App embeds in the theme editor sidebar.
3. Toggle ON the listed TAE block for the app.
4. Save.
5. Open the dev preview URL and verify the app's UI/behaviour renders.
6. If there's a legacy snippet still in the theme code for this app, file a follow-up ticket to remove it.

## Acceptance

- 10 of the listed 11 apps have their TAE block toggled ON in dev theme 141168312419 admin
- Each verified rendering correctly on preview
- For any app whose legacy snippet still exists, a follow-up "remove <app> legacy snippet" ticket is filed
- One commit to this branch documenting which apps were toggled + any follow-up tickets filed

## Out of scope

- The 7 YELLOW apps (manual cleanup of mixed-support apps — separate ticket)
- The 4 RED apps (legacy embed only — separate ticket per app)
- Re:amaze defer (P2.4 covers; same app though)

title: P2.4: Defer Re:amaze + LoyaltyLion + Judge.me to user-interaction
description:
Goal: defer heavyweight app scripts (Re:amaze 213 KB, LoyaltyLion 266+100 KB) until the user interacts. Per app-compatibility-matrix.md, both have native defer-on-interaction support.

## Steps

1. **Re:amaze**: enable "Load on interaction" toggle in Re:amaze dashboard. After enable, verify chat icon still appears (likely a placeholder/proxy is shown until interaction). Test: open dev preview → confirm Re:amaze doesn't load until chat icon is clicked.

2. **LoyaltyLion**: per app-compat matrix #11 + #12, LoyaltyLion ships two TAE blocks (`embed-sdk` and `embed-main`). Verify LL admin dashboard offers a defer-on-interaction toggle. If not, defer the SDK with a manual wrapper:
   ```js
   var lionTrigger = function () { /* lazy-load LL SDK */ };
   document.addEventListener('mouseenter', lionTrigger, { once: true });
   document.addEventListener('touchstart', lionTrigger, { once: true, passive: true });
   document.addEventListener('scroll', lionTrigger, { once: true, passive: true });
   ```

3. **Judge.me**: their TAE block (`judgeme_core`, GREEN #5) plus a "deferred loader + static stars" theme integration. Audit whether both load simultaneously (double-load risk per matrix note). Rely on TAE only if possible.

4. Measure TBT impact via PSI n=3 before/after each app's defer.

## Acceptance

- Re:amaze loads on interaction only (verified visually + via Network tab on dev preview)
- LoyaltyLion deferred (either via vendor toggle or manual wrapper)
- Judge.me audited for double-load; one source removed if duplicate
- Combined TBT improvement ≥ 300ms (lab, n=3, mobile)

## Out of scope

- Re:amaze defer skill (bd `hairmnl-theme-meu` is a parallel epic — may overlap; coordinate before starting)
- Klaviyo defer (Web Pixels covers most use-cases)

title: P2.3: Web Pixels Manager migration — GTM + Klaviyo + Elevar events
description:
Goal: migrate GTM, Klaviyo, and Elevar event tracking from synchronous `content_for_header` / hardcoded scripts to Web Pixels Manager (Shopify-native, runs in sandboxed worker — zero main-thread cost).

## Context

Per `os2-migration/perf-baseline-comparison.md`, third-party scripts contribute ~3.6 MB on mobile (P6 live). GTM alone is 618 KB. Klaviyo is 120 KB always-on. Elevar is 2.6s reported latency.

P8's Web Pixels Manager (OS 2.0 feature P6 cannot use) runs these in a sandboxed worker. Custom events go through `analytics.publish()` API; standard events fire automatically.

## Steps

1. Inventory current GTM tags + Klaviyo + Elevar events firing from theme code (snippets, layout, custom-theme.js).
2. For each: classify as standard event (page_view, checkout_started, etc.) OR custom event (engagement, scroll_depth, etc.).
3. Standard events: rely on Shopify's built-in tracking (no theme code needed).
4. Custom events: implement via Web Pixels custom pixel in Shopify admin (Settings → Customer events → Add custom pixel). Pixel code goes in admin, not theme.
5. Remove the theme-side `content_for_header` GTM container snippet (or shrink to just the GTM bootstrap if pixels can't fully replace it).

## Constraints

- Klaviyo: their TAE block (#4 GREEN in app-compat matrix) handles onsite popups. Web Pixels handles event reporting.
- Elevar: their TAE block (#6 YELLOW) is reported as 2.6s latency — may need to defer further.

## Acceptance

- ≥ 50% of currently-theme-side GTM tag triggers moved to Web Pixels Manager
- `content_for_header` GTM payload reduced (measure via Network tab — compare before/after weight)
- Standard events (page_view, etc.) verified firing in GA4 / Klaviyo dashboards
- TBT improvement measured (lab, n=3): aim for ≥ 200ms

## Out of scope

- Migrating away from GTM entirely (separate epic)
- BSS B2B's event tracking (BSS being uninstalled)

title: P2.2: Replace heavy vendor deps in custom-theme.js with native APIs (first vendor)
description:
Goal: replace heavy vendor dependencies in `custom-theme.js` with native browser APIs that P8 already provides.

## Vendor → native replacements

| Vendor | LoC est. | P8 native equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| `sticky-js` | ~400 | `position: sticky` (already done in `hairmnl-custom.js` section 1) |
| `swiper` + `dom7` + `ssr-window` | ~3,500 | P8 ships **Flickity** (used by stock slideshow) OR native `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` |
| `sweetalert2` | ~1,200 | native `<dialog>` element + small CSS |
| `he` (HTML entities) | ~150 | native `DOMParser` (already used in `hairmnl-custom.js` section 5) |

## Steps

1. For each vendor in `custom-theme.js`, identify the callsites that USE it (callsites identified in P2.1 inventory).
2. Replace each callsite with the native equivalent.
3. Once all callsites for a vendor are migrated, remove the vendor's bundled code from `custom-theme.js`.
4. Target ordering: easiest-first (he/DOMParser → sticky-js → sweetalert2 → swiper).

## Important constraint

`assets/custom-theme.js` is **mostly** vendor-bundled but in some sections vendor + hand-written code is interleaved. Be surgical.

## OC offload feasibility

**Hybrid**: CC does the architecture per-vendor (which callsites use it, what's the native pattern). OC senior-dev does the bulk callsite replacement once the pattern is established. Free-tier scope discipline: one vendor migration per dispatch.

## Acceptance

- One of the four vendor removals lands as a single PR
- TBT improvement of ≥ 100ms (lab, n=3) measured before/after on /
- Verify the affected feature still works visually (slideshow / alerts / etc.)
- Total `custom-theme.js` shrinks by ≥ 1000 LoC (one vendor removed)

## Out of scope

- Removing ALL four vendors in one ticket (file follow-up tickets if first removal succeeds)
- Replacing P8's own theme.js dependencies (prohibited file)

title: P2.1: custom-theme.js inventory + per-template usage audit
description:
Goal: quantify which behaviours in `assets/custom-theme.js` (re-enabled by commit `c108dc7`) are actually wired up to which P8 templates. The file is ~9,100 LoC; per the P4 spike report, ~750 LoC is hand-written and ~8,000 LoC is bundled vendor (sticky-js, sweetalert2, swiper, dom7, he, ssr-window).

## Steps

1. Read `assets/custom-theme.js` once end-to-end. Build an inventory of:
   - Hand-written behaviour blocks (search for "// ====" or named functions or `(function() { 'use strict'; })()`)
   - Vendor libraries bundled inside
   - For each behaviour: what DOM selectors it touches, what events it binds
2. Map each behaviour to the templates where its selectors actually exist on the rendered DOM:
   - Use a one-pass grep across `templates/*.json`, `sections/*.liquid`, `snippets/*.liquid` for each selector
3. Output: a markdown table (in `os2-migration/custom-theme-js-audit.md`) — Behaviour | Templates Used | Selectors | Vendor Deps | LoC | Removable?

## Output deliverable

`os2-migration/custom-theme-js-audit.md` with the table + a "Removable behaviours" section listing the LoC subtotal.

## Acceptance

- Inventory table covers every behaviour in custom-theme.js (no "misc" buckets)
- "Removable" subtotal estimated as LoC + KB
- Ranked recommendation: which 3 behaviours to remove FIRST for largest TBT reduction

## Out of scope

- Actually removing the behaviours (Phase 2.2)
- The `assets/hairmnl-custom.js` file (separate audit in P2.5)
notes:
2026-05-18 INCREMENT (OC swarm chain).

## What landed
- 1 NEW file: os2-migration/custom-theme-js-audit.md (139 LoC)
- Comprehensive inventory: 10 behaviors, 6 vendor libs, per-template parse overhead table
- ~3,216 LoC removable, ~94% wasted JS on home/cart/brand-collection
- Top 3 TBT recommendations:
  1. Swiper async-chunk — ~250KB savings, ~150-200ms TBT
  2. SweetAlert2 removal — ~128KB savings, ~80-120ms TBT
  3. sticky-js → CSS position:sticky — ~17KB, ~10-20ms TBT
- Commit: 04e6f9e, pushed to origin/main

## QA
PASS — all 10 behaviors accounted for, vendor LoC estimates realistic, no theme files modified.

## Remaining
This audit feeds P2.2 (vendor removal) and P2.5 (tree-shaking). No deploy needed for doc.
OC swarm completed audit doc os2-migration/custom-theme-js-audit.md (139 lines) on main directly. Key findings: 88% vendor code, 8% custom behaviors, 4% boilerplate. Removable: SweetAlert2 (2,631 LoC), sticky-js (344 LoC). Async-loadable: Swiper (4,296 LoC). Total ~3,216 LoC removable from custom-theme.js (~94% wasted JS on most templates). NOTE: This audit applies to claude/p4-pathb-cc's loading of custom-theme.js. On main, custom-theme.js is NOT loaded by layout/theme.liquid — so the audit's findings inform whether/when to ever load it (answer: probably never, except specific async chunks).

title: P1.5: Defer 6 of the 7 newly-wired CSS files (preload + onload swap)
description:
Goal: defer 5 of the 7 CSS files added by tonight's `7d4c71c` commit to non-render-blocking loads. They're currently all `stylesheet_tag` (synchronous, render-blocking).

## Files added tonight to `layout/theme.liquid`

1. `cart.css` — free-shipping progress bar (cart drawer + /cart page). **KEEP render-blocking** (cart drawer can appear above-fold via ATC).
2. `deferred-templates.css` — blog cards, search dropdown, account tables, password page. **DEFER** (none are above-fold on homepage).
3. `deferred-extras.css` — Bold Upsell modals, BCPO product options, Font Awesome icons. **DEFER** (modals trigger on interaction; icons can fade in).
4. `aos.css` — scroll animations. Depends on P1.4 decision. **DEFER** (animations are below-fold by nature).
5. `plyr.css` — video player. **DEFER** (videos trigger on interaction or are below-fold).
6. `bold-brain-widget.css` — recommendation widget. **DEFER** (widget appears below product details).
7. `tiny.content.min.css` — TinyIMG CMS content styles. **DEFER** (CMS content is mostly below-fold).

## Steps

1. Convert 6 of the 7 to async-load pattern:
   ```liquid
   <link rel="preload" as="style" href="{{ 'deferred-templates.css' | asset_url }}" onload="this.onload=null;this.rel='stylesheet'">
   <noscript><link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ 'deferred-templates.css' | asset_url }}"></noscript>
   ```
2. Keep `cart.css` as `stylesheet_tag` (render-blocking) — drawer is above-fold on first paint via mini-cart icon.
3. Verify visual identity: open dev theme, scroll through homepage / collection / PDP / brand pages, confirm no styling regressions (give CSS files ~1s to async-load).

## OC offload feasibility

OC-friendly once spec'd. Single file edit (layout/theme.liquid). ≤ 20 LoC change.

## Acceptance

- 6 CSS files load via preload-pattern (async); 1 (cart.css) remains render-blocking
- Lighthouse "Eliminate render-blocking resources" warning drops by 6 lines
- No visible visual regression on any template
- kt0 lint clean
notes:
2026-05-18 INCREMENT (OC swarm chain).

## What landed
- layout/theme.liquid: CSS defer pattern was ALREADY in place before this dispatch
- 6 preload+onload patterns with noscript fallbacks, cart.css render-blocking
- No new commits needed for this chain — acceptance criteria already met

## QA
PASS — 6 preload patterns confirmed, cart.css still render-blocking, kt0 clean.

## Remaining
Deploy to dev theme 141168312419 and verify via Lighthouse 'Eliminate render-blocking resources' audit.

title: P1.4: AOS layout-shift fix — gate, replace, or remove
description:
Goal: zero out CLS from Animate-on-Scroll (AOS) — tonight's commit `7d4c71c` newly wired `aos.css`, and AOS init was already in `layout/theme.liquid:91-93`. AOS elements start `opacity:0; translateY(20px)` and animate in on scroll, which can register as layout shift if the translated state takes a different layout slot.

## Decision tree

1. **First**: measure AOS's actual CLS contribution via DevTools (gate to `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` first to compare).
2. If CLS contribution from AOS is < 0.05: gate behind `prefers-reduced-motion` and ship.
3. If CLS contribution is ≥ 0.05: replace AOS with native IntersectionObserver + CSS-only `opacity` fade (no `translate`). Initial state is in-flow, just invisible.
4. If AOS is decorative and rarely shipped: remove the AOS init + the `aos.css` link entirely. Simpler.

## Files

- assets/aos.css (consider removing or replacing)
- layout/theme.liquid (lines ~91-93: AOS init code; line ~354: `{{ 'aos.css' | asset_url | stylesheet_tag }}` added tonight)
- POSSIBLY assets/hairmnl-custom.js (new section if replacing AOS with native impl)

## Acceptance

- AOS CLS contribution ≤ 0.02
- Either: AOS still working (with `prefers-reduced-motion` gate OR replaced impl) OR completely removed (decision documented)
- kt0 lint clean
- No visible visual regression on AOS-using sections

## Out of scope

- Other CLS sources (Phase 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.5)

title: P1.3: Font CLS — size-adjust/ascent-override for BasisGrotesquePro
description:
Goal: eliminate font-swap CLS by matching `BasisGrotesquePro-Regular` metrics to the UA fallback font.

## Context

`BasisGrotesquePro-Regular` is loaded with `font-display: swap`. During the swap window (after font loads), the layout reflows because the web font's character metrics differ from the UA fallback (default sans-serif). This contributes to CLS.

Tonight's commit `7ac5d3a` fixed the `:root { ---font-stack-* }` declarations (trailing comma bug), but did NOT address swap-metric mismatch.

## Steps

1. Measure: open Chrome DevTools, Network tab → throttle to Slow 4G → reload → record the layout shift caused by `BasisGrotesquePro-Regular` swap-in.
2. Compute `size-adjust`, `ascent-override`, `descent-override`, `line-gap-override` values that make `BasisGrotesquePro-Regular` *visually identical* to the fallback at the same `font-size`. Tools: https://fontmatcher.dev/ or manual measurement.
3. Apply the overrides as additional `@font-face` declarations after the existing font definitions (in `snippets/custom-fonts.liquid` or `snippets/font-settings-inline.liquid`).
4. Alternative: change `font-display: optional` for non-critical weights (italic, bold) — browser uses fallback if font doesn't load in 100ms first-paint window. Acceptable since fallback is visually close.

## Acceptance

- CLS contribution from font swap ≤ 0.01 (DevTools Performance recording)
- No visible visual regression (compare screenshots before/after)
- Hairline-shifted body text on first paint is acceptable; do not over-tune at the cost of looking wrong

## Out of scope

- `SelfModern` heading font (P6 already has `font-display: optional` per font-settings-inline.liquid; keep)
- New font families

title: P1.2: Image dimension audit — width/height attrs on all grid/hero/banner images
description: Goal: every `` in product cards, hero, banner, brand sections has explicit `width` and `height` attributes (browser reserves aspect-ratio box pre-load). ## Steps 1. Run Lighthouse "Image elements do not have explicit width and height" audit against /, /collections/best-sellers, /products/, /collections/loreal-professionnel. 2. For each flagged image, find the rendering snippet and add `width="…" height="…"` to the `` tag (use natural pixel dimensions OR computed slot dimensions, not display dimensions). 3. Snippets likely needing updates: product-grid-item.liquid, product-grid-item-branded.liquid, header-block.liquid, slideshow.liquid, brand-collection.liquid image renders. 4. Use Liquid `image_url`'s width/height helpers where available; fall back to hardcoded numeric values from product/collection metafields when not. ## OC offload feasibility This is **OC-friendly**: well-spec'd, multi-file mechanical edits. Bd ticket can be sent via `/offload` once P0.4 confirms scope. Caveat: ≤ 4 snippet files per dispatch (free tier discipline). ## Acceptance - Zero "image without explicit dimensions" warnings on the 5 templates in Lighthouse desktop audit - kt0 lint clean - All 5 templates render visually identical (spot-check screenshots) - Push to dev theme 141168312419 ## Out of scope - Image format conversion (already done — WebP shipped) - Lazy loading attribute (already done)
notes: 2026-05-18 INCREMENT (OC swarm chain). ## What landed - 2 files changed: sections/brand-collection.liquid (25 images) + snippets/product-grid-item.liquid (1 image) - Added explicit width/height to all 26 tags missing dimensions - Liquid .width/.height for image_picker sources; hardcoded for static icons - Commit: 04e6f9e, pushed to origin/main ## QA PASS — valid Liquid, all images now dimensioned, kt0 clean (370 files, 0 violations), no prohibited files touched. ## Remaining Deploy to dev theme 141168312419 and verify via Lighthouse audit. OC swarm 04e6f9e (image dimensions: 26 tags) landed on main directly. Files: sections/brand-collection.liquid (25 images) + snippets/product-grid-item.liquid (1 noscript fallback). Pattern: width="{{ image.width }}" height="{{ image.height }}" via Shopify Liquid image metadata accessor. Verified diff during merge-gate.

title: P1.1: Hero/slider pre-init dimension reservation (slider C)
description:
Continuation of bd `hairmnl-theme-ehrm` (already closed, slider A+B verified live; slider C status was "ready to spec" per commit 43f2e15).

Goal: zero out CLS from the homepage hero/slider/tile-grid pre-init by reserving exact aspect-ratio space before JS/CSS apply.

## Steps

1. Verify the ehrm fix is in this branch (commit 1cd6ae4 "cap pre-init homepage slider height + extend IO rootMargin").
2. Add `aspect-ratio` reservation to slider C (the "For Frizz" / category tile grid on the homepage).
3. Capture before/after CLS via DevTools Performance recording on `/?preview_theme_id=141168312419`.

## Files

title: Phase 5 visual parity: audit P6 vs P8 css-overrides.liquid for missed rules
description: OC paid audit: diff /tmp/p6-snippets/snippets/css-overrides.liquid (P6 LIVE theme 131664707683) against working tree snippets/css-overrides.liquid (P8). Port any P6 rules missing from P8 with P5-parity comments. Hard cap 200 LoC added. Single-file scope: snippets/css-overrides.liquid only. See design for invalid-CSS patterns to watch for (trailing commas, empty var() refs). Phantom-completion guard: paste git show HEAD --stat before close.
design:
# Phase 5 visual parity: audit P6 vs P8 css-overrides.liquid for missed rules

## Background

Phase 4.M3 (closed as hairmnl-theme-2i8b.4) ported `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` from Pipeline 6 to Pipeline 8. During Phase 5 visual QA on 2026-05-18, two issues were found in the cart drawer area:
1. Cart drawer didn't open on add-to-cart (P6 → P8 UX regression) — FIXED in commit e81459f via `assets/hairmnl-custom.js` section 7.
2. Entire site rendered in Times serif because the CSS variables `---font-stack-body` and `---font-stack-heading` had trailing commas (Liquid `font.fallback_families` rendered empty), making `font-family` declarations invalid. FIXED in commit 7ac5d3a via four `:root { ---font-stack-* }` overrides in `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`.

These suggest the M3 port may have missed other P6 styling rules that look fine when settings happen to produce valid CSS but break on edge cases (or are simply absent from P8).

## Scope (RO + RW, file-disjoint with other tickets)

Read-only reference:
- `/tmp/p6-snippets/snippets/css-overrides.liquid` — Pipeline 6 LIVE version (theme 131664707683).

Modify (single file):
- `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` in working tree.

Do NOT modify:
- `assets/hairmnl-custom.js` (active cart-drawer fix)
- `snippets/css-variables.liquid` (P8 stock; do not patch upstream)
- Any layout/theme.liquid, sections/*, assets/*.css/.js
- `config/settings_data.json`

## Required steps

1. **Diff the two files structurally**: `diff /tmp/p6-snippets/snippets/css-overrides.liquid snippets/css-overrides.liquid` — note both additions in P8 (most are P4-parity additions, ignore those) and absences in P8 (rules present in P6 that didn't make it across in M3).

2. **For each P6-only rule (not present in P8)**:
   - Determine if the selector is still valid in P8 (the cart-drawer.liquid, header.liquid, footer.liquid are largely identical between themes).
   - If selector still applies: port the rule into the appropriate section of P8's `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`, preserving the P6 comment block above it.
   - If selector references a P6-only class (e.g., `.tousled-header__wrapper` may or may not still exist in P8): tag the port with `P5-VERIFY:` comment.

3. **Look specifically for invalid-CSS-prone patterns** anywhere in the file:
   - Trailing commas in lists (font-family, background, etc.)
   - Liquid interpolations that could resolve to empty strings inside a CSS declaration
   - `var(--name)` references to variables that aren't declared anywhere

4. **Document each ported rule** with a top-of-block `P5-parity` comment: what it does and which P6 selector it came from.

5. **kt0 lint must pass**: `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` exit 0 before close.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `diff /tmp/p6-snippets/snippets/css-overrides.liquid snippets/css-overrides.liquid` shows that every P6-only rule has either been ported or explicitly skipped with a `P5-SKIP:` comment in the bd notes (with reason).
- [ ] `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` exits 0 against the modified working tree.
- [ ] No invalid CSS patterns (trailing commas in font-family lists, empty `var()` references) introduced.
- [ ] git diff is clean — only `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` modified.
- [ ] Paste `git show HEAD --stat` into bd notes before close (phantom-completion guard).

## Out of scope

- Visual verification in browser (no browser available; that's the operator's morning task).
- Pushing to dev theme (operator will push after morning visual review).
- Touching `assets/*.css`, `assets/*.js`, sections, layout, or settings.
- Adding new snippet files.
- Changes to `css-variables.liquid` upstream.

## Output location

All edits go into `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`. Group new rules under a new section header:
```
/* ============================================================
 * Phase 5 parity audit (2026-05-18) — P6 rules missed in M3 port
 * ============================================================ */
```

## Stop conditions

If diff reveals >300 lines of missed rules, STOP and report — that's structural, not a port-audit; warrants a separate decomposition discussion. Hard cap: 200 LoC additions to `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Every P6-only rule ported OR explicitly P5-SKIP comment with reason. 2) python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py exits 0. 3) No invalid-CSS patterns introduced. 4) git diff clean (only snippets/css-overrides.liquid). 5) git show HEAD --stat pasted into bd notes.

title: Phase 4.M7: Lint + CLS audit pass on Pipeline 8 working tree (Wave D)
description: OFFLOAD to OC. After M1-M6 merge. Run scripts/check-overlay-css.py + CLS spot-checks per template (homepage, product, brand collection, blog index, article, cart). Output single report at os2-migration/p4-lint-report.md. Parallel-safe with CC Phase 5 visual QA.
design:
## Output path (explicit)

- `os2-migration/p4-lint-report.md` (the ONLY output file)
- **NO scripts modified.** **NO theme files touched.**

## What to do

After M1-M6 have all merged, run the project's pre-deploy lint suite against the Pipeline 8 working tree and document results.

**Lint commands to run:**

1. `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` — the kt0 CSS containment audit. Exit 0 = clean. Exit 1 = violation (each violation must be acknowledged with `kt0-OK` comment in the offending rule, or fixed).

2. CLS spot-checks: run `./scripts/run-psi.sh https://www.hairmnl.com/?preview_theme_id=<P4_DEV_THEME_ID> mobile 2` against the following template URLs (operator will provide the dev-theme ID at dispatch time):
   - Homepage `/`
   - A product page (`/products/davines-momo-shampoo` or operator's choice)
   - A brand collection page (`/collections/loreal-professionnel`)
   - The blog index (`/blogs/news`)
   - An article (operator picks)
   - Cart page (`/cart`)

3. Compare each CLS to the baseline numbers in `os2-migration/perf-baseline-comparison.md`. Flag any regressions.

## Report format

`os2-migration/p4-lint-report.md` structure:

```
# Phase 4 lint + CLS audit report

## kt0 CSS containment audit
- Result: PASS / FAIL with N violations
- Each violation: file:line + rule + acknowledgment status (kt0-OK present or not)

## CLS spot-checks per template
| Template | P6 baseline CLS | P4 dev CLS | Δ | Verdict |

## Recommendations
- List any blocks that should be re-opened (M3 follow-up if CLS regressions)
- List any kt0 violations that need fixes vs need `kt0-OK` comments
```

## Constraints

- DO NOT modify any theme files. Read-only audit + write the report.
- DO NOT modify the lint script.
- ≤1-2 hours of OC time. ≤120 LoC for the report.

## Acceptance

`os2-migration/p4-lint-report.md` committed with PASS/FAIL verdict + per-template CLS results. `git show HEAD --stat` in close notes.
acceptance_criteria: os2-migration/p4-lint-report.md committed with PASS/FAIL kt0 verdict + per-template CLS results vs P6 baseline. git show HEAD --stat in close notes.

title: Wave 0: Rationale / feasibility deck for stakeholders (HTML)
description:
Build a non-technical rationale / feasibility deck for stakeholder alignment BEFORE Wave 1 research kicks off.

**Format:** single-file HTML, no external dependencies (inline CSS, no JS framework), slide-deck layout (one full-screen section per topic), print-friendly, GH-hostable via GitHub Pages.

**Path:** `docs/os2-migration/feasibility.html` + `docs/os2-migration/README.md` (5-line index).

**Audience:** Non-technical stakeholder running the business. Vendor names (Klaviyo, Reamaze, LimeSpot, etc.) are OK; ticket IDs / internal labels / file paths / tool names are NOT.

**Content (one slide each):** Cover; Where we are today; What could go wrong if we do nothing (5 risks, HIGH/MED/LOW tags); What's NOT a risk (counter-FUD); The choice we're making (prepare now, not migrate now); The 5 triggers; Cost of preparing; Cost of migrating (conditional); Benefits of preparing now; Timeline & next steps; What we'll know after prep; Update history.

**Update protocol post-Wave-0:** Each subsequent ticket that produces a number the deck cites (LoC, perf delta, vendor status) → update deck in place with a one-line entry in update-history slide. Single-file structure makes this a 5-minute edit.
notes:
Wave 0 deck shipped.

**Commit:** 63eef21 on branch claude/sleepy-volhard-7bf010 (pushed to origin)

**Files:**
- docs/os2-migration/feasibility.html  (587 lines, single-file HTML, inline CSS, no external deps)
- docs/os2-migration/README.md          (22 lines, directory index)

**Diff stat:**
```
 docs/os2-migration/README.md        |  22 ++
 docs/os2-migration/feasibility.html | 587 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 609 insertions(+)
```

**Deck contents (12 sections):** Cover; Where we are today; What could go wrong if we do nothing (5 risks, HIGH/MED/LOW pills); What is not a risk (counter-FUD); The choice we are making (wait / prepare / migrate comparison); The five triggers; Cost of preparing (1.5-2 weeks); Cost of migrating (3-6 weeks, conditional); Benefits of preparing now (5 bullets); Timeline & next steps; What we will know after preparing; Update history (v1.0 stamp).

**Audience compliance:** No bd IDs, file paths, internal labels (kt0, id2), past-bug references by ticket ID, or tool names in the user-facing copy. Vendor names (Klaviyo, Reamaze, LimeSpot, Elevar, Groupthought) kept since they're recognizable products.

**Hosting:** README links to htmlpreview.github.io fallback URL for raw GitHub preview. GH Pages can be enabled separately if desired.

**Update protocol:** Each subsequent ticket that produces a number the deck cites will refresh the deck in place via the update-history slide. Single-file structure keeps that to a ~5-min edit.

Closing as complete. Next: Wave 1 dispatch (P1.1 / P1.2 / P1.3 / P3.1 / P3.2) on user go-ahead.

description: Install Pipeline 8.1.1 as a fresh dev theme on creations-gdc.myshopify.com (do NOT publish; do NOT touch live theme 131664707683). Measure: LCP, CLS, INP, FCP, TTFB, total JS bytes, total CSS bytes, # render-blocking resources. Output: os2-migration/perf-baseline-comparison.md. notes:

**Theme used:** `141168312419` ("Pipeline 8 Working Demo") on creations-gdc.myshopify.com, unpublished. Confirmed bare V8.1.1 via shopify theme pull (blocks/ dir, section groups, stock P8 section types).

**PSI results (n=3 median):**
- Mobile: score 62 (+33 vs live 29), TBT 175ms (-93%), CLS 0.000 (-100%), FCP 2.81s
- Desktop: score 72 (+18 vs live 54), TBT 225ms (-87%), CLS 0.000
- Render-blocking: 0 (vs live: 2)
- Mobile bytes (adjusted, no preview-bar): 3.31 MB vs live 6.41 MB (-48%)
- After estimated +apps re-install: ~5.04 MB (~-21% vs live)
- LCP delta: inconclusive in lab (preview cache-state artifact); field-only verdict possible post-publish

**Headline takeaway:** Pipeline 8 collapses TBT and CLS — the metrics most tied to perceived performance. Page-byte savings are real but smaller after re-installing the legacy snippet-based apps (Klaviyo, Reamaze, GTM, Giftwrap, BSS — all absent from preview).

**Verdict section in doc** recommends sharpening Risk #2 in stakeholder deck to quantified TBT/CLS claim (not just "performance ceiling").

- Measured "+apps" column requires installing TAE versions of Klaviyo/Reamaze/Giftwrap/BSS on dev theme (~2-3h per app)
- Minimum branding config in theme settings (not needed — comparison stands without)
