title: PDP CLS+INP — Judge.me preview badge reservation + gallery zoom yield (884 URLs)
description:
GSC mobile CWV report (5/10) shows 884 of 1,231 CLS-failing URLs (72%) are query-param variants of /products/kerastase-blond-absolu-purple-shampoo-250ml. Same product also accounts for 884/884 (100%) of the INP > 200ms cohort. RUM CWV dashboard top_inp_targets confirms: PDP image gallery (flickity-slider > product__media > lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper > img.srcset) is the #2 INP target site-wide.

Two distinct fixes, both PDP-wide (benefit every product page with reviews + every product page period):

## CLS — Judge.me preview badge space reservation (top of PDP)

The product page renders <div class='jdgm-widget jdgm-prev-badge' data-id='...' data-number-of-reviews='21' data-average-rating='4.95'> just below the product title. The div is initially EMPTY in server HTML — Judge.me's loader.js hydrates it with stars + "21 reviews" text post-load.

Without space reservation, the badge grows from 0 → ~24px tall, pushing the price, variant selector, ATC button, and entire below-fold content down. This is a TOP-OF-PAGE shift, which CLS scores heavily because it shifts a large area of the viewport.

Existing CSS only covers bottom-of-PDP medals wrapper (snippets/css-overrides.liquid:168). The top-of-page snippet has no reservation.

## INP — PDP image gallery zoom handler

The flickity main gallery's mobile-zoom-wrapper is responsible for 27 INP events in the last 7d (target: div.flickity-slider>div.product__media.is-selected>div>div.lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper>img.srcset). When user taps to zoom, the synchronous tap-handler does too much work (init zoom-pswp lightbox, load high-res image, animate). Goal: bring single-tap p75 INP under 200ms.
design:
## Phase A — CLS fix (low-risk, high-leverage)

Add to snippets/css-overrides.liquid (after the existing jdgm-medals-wrapper rule at line 168):

```css
/* CLS fix YYYY-MM-DD <bd-id> — Judge.me preview badge top-of-PDP shift.
   <div class="jdgm-widget jdgm-prev-badge"> renders empty at PDP top
   below product title; loader.js injects stars + "N reviews" → grows
   from 0 → ~24px → top-of-page shift → 884 URLs failing CLS on
   kerastase-blond-absolu PDP grouping. Reserve typical badge height
   so the hydration is paint-only, not layout. contain:layout
   isolates internal star-count changes from cascading. */
.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge {
  display: inline-block;
  min-height: 24px;
  min-width: 100px;
  contain: layout;
}
```

Files: snippets/css-overrides.liquid (CSS-only, ~10 lines).

Verification: 24h post-deploy, GSC validation should rerun and the 884 affected URLs should drop out of CLS-failing cohort.

## Phase B — INP fix (more invasive)

Investigation needed:
1. Identify which JS handler is the synchronous slow path on mobile-zoom-wrapper tap. Likely candidates:
   - flickity click → cellChange recompute (heavy if many slides)
   - zoom-pswp PhotoSwipe init on first tap (lazy, 1-time cost)
   - mobile-zoom-wrapper handler in custom-theme.js (no sourcemaps — deferred per 3sr)
2. Strategies:
   - Defer PhotoSwipe library import until idle (currently loaded eagerly?)
   - Yield with requestIdleCallback inside the tap handler so the layout/paint can occur within INP budget
   - Use scheduler.postTask if available (not all browsers)
3. Validation: synthetic Lighthouse PWA audit + real INP rerun in 24h.

Files: assets/zoom-pswp.js (or wherever zoom is initialized), assets/theme.dev.js + assets/theme.js (mirror discipline per .opencode_hints).

Phase A unblocks GSC quickly. Phase B is a separate dispatch.
acceptance_criteria:
## Phase A acceptance
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid contains a `.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge` rule with min-height/min-width/contain:layout, following the existing CLS fix comment convention (date + bd-id + reason).
- Diff is CSS-only, no Liquid or JS changes.
- Pushed to draft, then live (smoke: visit kerastase PDP on mobile incognito, badge area pre-reserved before Judge.me hydrates).
- 24h post-deploy: GSC validation rerun on the CLS-mobile-0.1 issue should show the 884 kerastase-blond-absolu URLs drop out (or shift to needs-improvement → good).

## Phase B acceptance (separate dispatch)
- Mobile gallery first-tap INP p75 < 200ms (measured via web-vitals reporter, debug_target containing mobile-zoom-wrapper).
- No regression in zoom UX (lightbox still opens, images still high-res).
- assets/theme.js minified mirror updated to match assets/theme.dev.js source change per .opencode_hints mirror discipline.

title: RUM dashboard LCP regression is GTM instrumentation, not perf — diff V141 vs V140 + fix
description:
RUM CWV dashboard shows LCP-good dropped from 84% (May 7) to 66% (May 10) — but this is an INSTRUMENTATION regression, not a real perf regression.

Evidence:
- N halved between May 7 → May 8: LCP 25,497→12,046 (-53%); CLS -46%; FCP -60%; TTFB -33%; INP -1% (FLAT).
- Only INP (interaction-fired) preserved volume; the 4 load-time metrics dropped 33-60%.
- top_templates.LCP shows 95.7% of events have template="(not set)" — custom dimension parameter is no longer being captured.
- Dashboard query at scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:384 silently drops events with metric_rating="(not set)" by only counting good/needs-improvement/poor — masks the real issue.
- Timing aligns exactly with c03ab92 (l3g GTM fix, Container Version 141 published May 6-7).

Hypothesis: GTM Container Version 141 republish inadvertently affected the web-vitals GA4 tag(s) — likely either trigger config or eventSettingsTable param mappings. Need to diff V141 vs V140 to confirm.
design:
## Investigation + fix plan

### Phase 1 — Diagnosis (read-only via GTM API)
1. List all GTM container versions; locate V140 (pre-fix) and V141 (post-fix l3g publish).
2. Diff V141 vs V140: enumerate every tag/trigger/variable that changed. Should be exactly tag 776 + DLVs 773/774/775 if the l3g fix was clean.
3. Locate the web-vitals tags (firing on Custom Event = LCP|CLS|INP|FCP|TTFB). Inspect their firing triggers, eventSettingsTable parameter mappings (especially `metric_rating` and `template`), and tag IDs.
4. Confirm whether the metric_rating + template params reach GA4 via DebugView or via the V141 export inspection.

### Phase 2 — Fix in fresh workspace
- Create a NEW workspace (NOT 197 or 190 or any leftover) from current live (V141).
- For each broken web-vitals tag:
  - Restore the broken parameter mappings to their V140 form.
- Create + publish a new Container Version. Coordinator self-publishes since the user authorized autonomous fix.

### Phase 3 — Defensive dashboard fix
- scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:384 — count events with metric_rating="(not set)" into a separate "unknown" bucket (or surface count alongside totals) so silent param failures are visible going forward.

### Phase 4 — Verify
- Wait 24h; rerun dashboard. LCP-good should rebound to ~84%; template column should populate.
- After 7d, legacy "(not set)" rows age out of the rolling window.

## Out of scope
- Re-publishing the l3g fix (it was intentional and correct — JS error tag still needs trigger 772).
- Further changes to the 3 orphan-trigger tags (4r8, t4m, miy) — separate bd issues.
acceptance_criteria:
- GTM Container Version diff (V141 vs V140) captured in bd notes; smoking gun confirmed.
- Web-vitals tag(s) restored to working state via new container version published from a fresh workspace.
- Dashboard query at line 384 updated to count or surface (not set) ratings (defensive).
- 24h post-fix dashboard refresh shows LCP-good >= 80% and template column populated with real Liquid template names (index, product, blog, etc.).
- bd issue closed with phantom-completion guard (git show HEAD --stat in notes).

title: /cart INP regression — gjv fix did not move needle (41% poor 4d post-fix vs 30% pre-fix)
description: gjv (commit 3a22d94, 2026-05-05) scoped cart--loading class to active row to fix style-recalc. Field data 4d post-fix shows /cart INP poor% WORSE: May 4 30% → May 9 41%. Hypothesis: third-party widget orchestration (LimeSpot, Klaviyo, BOGOS, Reamaze, sticky-bar) is the dominant cost, not the style-recalc gjv addressed. Need DevTools investigation on /cart with line items + qty stepper interaction.
design:
## Goal
Diagnose why /cart INP is worse 4 days post-gjv (41% poor) than the May 4 pre-fix baseline (30% poor). gjv (commit 3a22d94, 2026-05-05) scoped `cart--loading` class to active row instead of page wrapper — should have dropped style-recalc from O(N) to O(1) on each stepper click. Field data shows the fix did NOT move the needle.

## Hypothesis
Third-party widget orchestration on /cart is the dominant INP cost, not our cart-stepper handler:
- LimeSpot SDK (gated 2026-04-28 but cart still has widget mounts)
- Klaviyo signup popup (deferred 20 sec + 60% scroll, but still hydrates)
- BOGOS free-gift recompute on cart change (see bd "bogos-error" for separate bug)
- Sticky add-to-cart bar
- Reamaze widget hydration

The gjv fix addressed the WRONG thing — it fixed style-recalc from a Liquid-level class addition, but the real INP cost is JS ORCHESTRATION across these widgets when cart state changes.

## Investigation steps (Brave + Claude-in-Chrome MCP, mobile emulation)
1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/cart with at least 2 line items in mobile emulation (Moto G Power, Slow 4G).
2. DevTools → Performance → Record. Click qty stepper. Stop recording.
3. In the timeline, identify the LONG TASK that immediately follows the click event.
4. Expand the long task: which JS frame dominates? Which third-party origin?
5. Cross-check with GA4 RUM `debug_target` for /cart — look for the most-attributed selectors beyond `#shopify-section-template--XXX__main` (which is the generic cart body wrapper).
6. Repeat for: drawer open, line-item remove, freegift add.

## Likely fixes (ranked by leverage)
- A. Defer BOGOS recompute to requestIdleCallback (don't block first paint after cart change)
- B. Debounce qty stepper clicks (250ms — most users are spam-clicking)
- C. Yield to main thread between cart-update tasks (`scheduler.postTask()` or setTimeout 0)
- D. Disable widget re-init on cart state change (rely on the widget's own state hydration)

## Files likely to modify (post-investigation)
- assets/theme.dev.js + theme.js — CartItems class, drawer logic
- snippets/* — third-party widget includes
- Possibly snippets/limespot.liquid, snippets/klaviyo-signup-script.liquid, BOGOS-related snippets

## Out of scope
- Reverting the gjv fix (the row-scoping is still correct; just insufficient)
- Major refactor of cart UX
- Vertex cutover (separate, gated on Decision 2)

## Wait for clean signal
The full 7-day RUM window post-fix closes 2026-05-12. Investigate via DevTools NOW (synthetic) and confirm the directional signal with the May 12+ data.
acceptance_criteria:
After investigation + targeted fix:
- /cart INP poor% drops from 41% → ≤15% (matches site-wide 81% INP good)
- CrUX mobile INP p75 holds at ≤251ms or improves
- No regression on cart functionality (qty steppers, drawer, line-item remove all work)
- Specific dominant blocker (per debug_target) documented in commit message + bd notes
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-05-10 — root cause identified.

## /cart INP attribution (last 14d, GA4 Data API)
37/41 (90%) of poor-INP events on /cart target the SAME selector chain:
  #shopify-section-template--XXX__main > div.cart__template > div.wrapper.pt2.pb4

The two different template--XXX values are just different Shopify section instances. The browser couldn't isolate a more specific child target — meaning the slow handler is bound far up the DOM tree (event delegation OR full-wrapper re-render).

## Static analysis — CartItems.updateCart() flow (assets/theme.dev.js:4116)

When user clicks a qty stepper, the click→paint critical path includes ALL of:

1. `cart.js` GET (read current state)               ← network 1
2. `cart/change.js` POST (mutate)                   ← network 2
3. `theme:cart:change` event dispatch — TWO other listeners run (line 1716 = header cart count badge, line 2572 = drawer state sync)
4. `?section_id=api-cart-items` GET (re-render HTML)  ← network 3
5. `this.items.innerHTML = …` swap (full DOM re-creation)
6. `initQuantity()` re-binds ALL input + remove handlers
7. `initUpsell()` runs ProductAddButton constructor
8. `?section_id=api-cart-subtotal` GET (re-render subtotal) ← network 4
9. `this.subtotal.innerHTML = …` swap

**4 sequential network round-trips + 2 innerHTML swaps + 3-5 listener cascades, all on the click→paint critical path.** That's the INP cost.

The gjv fix (commit 3a22d94) optimized step 6's per-row class addition (O(N) → O(1)) — a real but TINY contribution to the total chain. It correctly fixed what it targeted but didn't address the dominant cost.

## Recommended Phase 2 fix (bounded, ~50 LoC)

Replace 4 sequential fetches with 1 using Shopify's bulk-sections cart API:

```
POST /cart/change.js
Body: { id, quantity, sections: "api-cart-items,api-cart-subtotal", sections_url: "/cart" }
Response: { ...cart, sections: { "api-cart-items": "<html>", "api-cart-subtotal": "<html>" } }
```

Eliminates: 1 GET (cart.js prefetch), 1 GET (api-cart-items), 1 GET (api-cart-subtotal). Keeps: 1 POST. Returns the new cart state AND both rendered section HTML in one round-trip.

**Files:**
- assets/theme.dev.js — `updateCart` method (~lines 4116-4169) + `loadForm`/`updateTotal`
- assets/theme.js — minified mirror (find by string `cart/change.js`)

**Expected impact (provisional):**
- /cart INP poor% drops from 41% → <20% (matches site-wide ~19% poor)
- Removes ~3 round-trips of network latency from the critical path
- Stacks additively with the BOGOS empty-cart gate shipped 2026-05-10 (commit c745875)

## Wait to dispatch

The BOGOS gate shipped today (commit c745875). Its effect on /cart INP won't be measurable until ~2026-05-17 (full 7-day post-fix RUM window). Recommend:
1. Wait until 2026-05-13 (3 days post-BOGOS-gate) — enough to see directional signal on whether BOGOS was material
2. If /cart INP poor% is still ≥30%, dispatch the bulk-sections-API fix
3. If it dropped significantly (e.g. <20%), close o0z and let the BOGOS gate do its work

## Other potential follow-ups (defer)
- Debounce qty stepper for rapid clicks (~10 LoC, in initInputs)
- Defer `theme:cart:change` listeners to requestIdleCallback (~5 LoC)
- Avoid full innerHTML swap (only update changed quantity field) — too invasive for now

title: Investigate + fix mobile LCP on top friction blog posts (47–63% poor)
description: Top 2 friction pages by LCP poor% are blog posts on /blogs/hair-education/. Article hero preload already in place (9hw). LCP element must be something else — likely the first in-body image, not the hero. Need DevTools investigation in Brave to confirm, then ship a targeted preload/fetchpriority extension.
design:
## Goal
Identify the actual LCP element on the two top friction blog posts and ship a targeted fix. Article hero preload (issue 9hw, 2026-05-04) is already in place — so the LCP element on these blog posts is something OTHER than the hero.

## Affected pages (from GA4 RUM 7d, latest snapshot 2026-05-09)
- /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-the-transformative-power-of-purple-shampoo (49 poor / 77 total = 63% poor LCP)
- /blogs/hair-education/rinse-out-conditioner-vs-leave-in-conditioner-what-s-the-difference (41 poor / 88 total = 47% poor)

## Investigation steps (Brave + Claude-in-Chrome MCP, mobile emulation)
1. Open each URL in Brave with mobile (Moto G Power, Slow 4G) emulation.
2. Open DevTools → Performance → Record a fresh page load.
3. Identify the LCP candidate from the timeline (highlight the LCP marker → element).
4. Hypothesis to test: largest body image is the LCP, NOT the hero. Article images are 480x480 from the post-processor — likely too small for hero-replacement role on mobile.
5. Cross-check with GA4 RUM event params (event=largest_contentful_paint, debug_target field).

## Likely fixes (ranked by leverage, choose after investigation)
- A. Inject fetchpriority="high" + preload on the FIRST in-body image (not just the article.image hero) — extends 9hw pattern.
- B. Lazy-load below-fold body images more aggressively (current: native loading=lazy; could add IntersectionObserver-driven decode hints).
- C. If LCP is text-on-cover (overlay over hero), reserve aspect-ratio earlier.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid (extend post-processor to inject fetchpriority on first image)
- sections/article.liquid (template-level preload if first body image's URL can be derived server-side)

## NOT dispatchable
This requires DevTools-based investigation which OpenCode (headless) cannot perform. Keep on CC + browser MCP.
acceptance_criteria:
After fix:
- /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-...purple-shampoo poor% drops below 30% (was 63%)
- /blogs/hair-education/rinse-out-...vs-leave-in poor% drops below 30% (was 47%)
- CrUX field LCP p75 mobile improves (currently 3.375s; aim ≤2.5s within 28-day window post-fix)
- No CLS regression (top friction CLS pages unchanged)
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-05-10 — root cause confirmed via GA4 RUM attribution. ## LCP attribution (last 14d, both pages) Purple shampoo blog (67 poor LCP events): 28 events div.blog__article>div.blog__article__content.rte.center>div>img ← FIRST IN-BODY IMAGE 18 events div.article__card.section--image.text--white>div.image__fill.fade-in-child>picture>img.fade-in-child ← hero (already preloaded via 9hw) 16 events h1.blog__article-title ← title text 2 events drawer-cart, header logo, etc. Rinse-out vs leave-in blog (69 poor LCP events): 31 events div.blog__article__content.rte > div > img ← FIRST IN-BODY IMAGE 10 events blog body

text 10 events blog title h1 10 events hero picture/img ## Conclusion Hypothesis confirmed. The 9hw fix correctly preloads the hero image (the `article__card` element in attribution), but the FIRST IN-BODY IMAGE is becoming LCP on 42-45% of events. Why: the hero has a CSS fade-in animation that delays its paint registration, while the first body image renders without animation and wins the "largest contentful element" race despite loading later. ## Fix scope (offload-ready) In snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid, the existing post-processor loop runs offset:1 over ` Date: Sun May 10 01:06:34 2026 +0200 fix(blog): fetchpriority=high + loading=eager on first body image (closes 7tq) GA4 RUM attribution (last 14d) on the two top-friction blog posts showed the first in-body image is the LCP candidate on 42-45% of poor LCP events — outpacing the hero (already preloaded via 9hw). Why: hero has CSS fade-in animation that delays its paint registration; the unanimated body image wins the largest-contentful- element race despite loading later. Fix: inside snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid post-processor loop, branch on forloop.first. First iteration injects fetchpriority=high + loading=eager. Subsequent iterations inject loading=lazy (existing behavior). decoding=async stays universal. Stacks additively with the hero preload from 9hw. Brave smoke test verified on /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-...: 1st body img: fetchpriority=high, loading=eager, decoding=async, width=480 2nd body img: fetchpriority=null, loading=lazy, decoding=async 3rd body img: fetchpriority=null, loading=lazy 11 images total — only the 1st gets priority hints. Out of scope (deferred to follow-up if needed): - Disabling hero CSS fade-in on blogs (alternative path) - Body image responsive srcset (existing dimension injection covers CLS) - First-image priority on non-Shopify-CDN images (current logic gates injection on _w/_h dimension parsing — covers Shopify CDN _NNNxNNN pattern which is what the affected pages use) Expected impact (post-fix RUM, full window 2026-05-17): - Purple shampoo blog poor% drops from 63% → <30% - Rinse-out vs leave-in blog poor% drops from 47% → <25% - CrUX mobile field LCP p75 should improve over 28-day rolling window (currently 3.375s POOR, target ≤2.5s GOOD) Note on dispatch: tried via OC sub-orchestrator (/hh-orchestrate) twice; both attempts hung at the lead-architect step (free-tier minimax-m2.5 contention with concurrent dispatches in another project). Per CLAUDE.md 'two failed dispatches → escalate not retry', pulled back to CC. Fix is small enough (~10 LoC effective) that CC turnaround was faster anyway. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 .beads/issues.jsonl | 20 ++++++++++---------- snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid | 24 ++++++++++++++++++++---- 2 files changed, 30 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-) Pending RUM verification 2026-05-17 (full 7d post-fix window for blog poor% drop; 28d for CrUX field LCP).

title: Fix dashboard table overflow in multi-column cards

title: Fix dashboard table readability and snapshot freshness

Table style guide: all numeric headers/cells use class=num and right alignment, tabular figures, nowrap; tables live in horizontally scrollable wrappers.
notes: Verification so far: py_compile passed; render-only passed; source-aware load shows May 4/5 PSI retained; real refresh outside sandbox fetched CrUX, PSI mobile, and GA4 and appended 2026-05-06T21:21:45Z snapshot. CHART_DATA now labels May 01, May 04, May 05, May 06.

title: GTM tag 415 (Klaviyo Form Submission Listener) references orphan trigger 2147479553 — email automation revenue tracking broken
description: Tag 415 in GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD live v140 references trigger ID 2147479553 which does not exist anywhere in the container. Tag tracks form submission events for Klaviyo email flows. Without a valid trigger, the tag cannot fire — email automation revenue attribution is broken (won't credit emails for purchases triggered after form submissions). Same root cause as bd hairmnl-theme-l3g: trigger was deleted in workspace cleanup but dependent tags weren't updated. Investigate which trigger Klaviyo expects (likely a Custom Event for klaviyo form events, OR All Pages). Coordinate with marketing/email-ops before reconnecting.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS UPDATE 2026-05-10T10:50:17Z — no GTM fix needed; misdiagnosis in original bd.

Trigger ID 2147479553 is GTM's BUILT-IN 'All Pages' trigger. Built-in triggers don't appear in workspaces().triggers().list() (why prior session flagged it as orphan) but ARE compiled into the live container.

Evidence:
1. Live gtm.js?id=GTM-M4NKSBD shows tag 415 compiled with predicate 1 = '_eq macro=0 arg1=gtm.js' — fires on every page load.
2. Klaviyo's GTM setup guide (Analytics Mania klaviyo-forms recipe; developers.klaviyo.com): 'Set this tag to fire on all pages.' Listener tags must attach early on every page.
3. Tag HTML pushes dl_subscribe to dataLayer on submit; downstream trigger 409 (Event - Klaviyo Email Form Submit) fires Klaviyo tags. Chain intact.

If GA4/Klaviyo email-attribution numbers look wrong in reports, the root cause is NOT this tag's trigger. File a fresh bd with the GA4 data showing the gap.

References: gtm-audit.py:100-101, scripts/gtm-inspect-orphan-trio.py

title: GTM tag 12 (Conversion Linker) references orphan trigger 2147479553 — GA4 conversion attribution broken
description: Tag 12 (Conversion Linker) in GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD live v140 references trigger ID 2147479553 which does not exist anywhere in the container. Without a valid trigger, the Conversion Linker tag cannot fire. Impact: all GA4 conversion attribution broken — last-click attribution fails, GCLID/braid cookies not set, cross-domain GA4 tracking degraded. The trigger likely existed in the 2019 workspace but was deleted without updating dependent tags. Fix requires deciding which valid trigger to reconnect (likely 'All Pages' or 'Initialization - All Pages'). Investigate via GTM admin UI; coordinate with marketing on which trigger semantics make sense. References bd hairmnl-theme-l3g handoff doc.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS UPDATE 2026-05-10T10:50:17Z — no GTM fix needed; misdiagnosis in original bd.

Trigger ID 2147479553 = GTM's built-in All Pages trigger. Live container shows tag 12 (Conversion Linker, type=gclidw) compiled with predicate 1 (gtm.js page load).

Vendor doc check (Google support.google.com/tagmanager/answer/7549390): 'In most cases, you should use the All Pages trigger to ensure that the linker tag is active on any potential landing page.' Tag is wired exactly as Google's docs prescribe.

Conversion Linker writes gclid/gbraid/wbraid to first-party cookies on landing. If Google Ads conversion attribution is actually showing gaps in reports, the cause is downstream (Google Ads ↔ GA4 linking, campaign URL tagging, consent gating, GA4 signals config), not this tag's trigger.

References: gtm-audit.py:100-101, scripts/gtm-inspect-orphan-trio.py

title: Fix GTM JS Error tag - wrong trigger + event name mismatch
description:
The GA4 - JS Error Event tag (GTM-M4NKSBD, live version 140) is misconfigured, causing GA4 to show (not set) for all error_type/error_message/error_source dimensions despite 40K+ js_error events.

**Root cause (confirmed by live GTM inspection, 2026-05-06):**
- Tag fires on: "Initialization - All Pages" (fires at page load, not on errors)
- Event name sent to GA4: "js_error" (old name)
- Theme pushes: "hairmnl_js_error" with fields error_type/error_message/error_source

**Result:** tag fires on every page load with empty dimension values, NOT on actual JS errors. The real error events (hairmnl_js_error) never reach the tag because the trigger is wrong.

**Also found:** 3 other critical tags have orphaned trigger ID 2147479553 (Conversion Linker, Klaviyo Form Listener, TrafficGuard) — these also need investigation.

**Fix required (GTM, not theme):**
1. Change GA4 - JS Error Event trigger from "Initialization - All Pages" → "Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error" (trigger 772)
2. Verify eventName is correct in tag parameters
3. Fix the 3 other broken tags (2147479553) 
4. Publish to live
5. Update dashboard query to also capture "hairmnl_js_error" events

**Dashboard also needs updating** (scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py): the JS error query filters eventName=js_error, but theme now sends hairminl_js_error. Need _in filter for both event names.

**Files involved:**
- scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py (dashboard query update)
- layout/theme.liquid (already correct - do not change)
- GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD (workspace 197) - fix via GTM UI or API

**Context:** theme was migrated 2026-05-02 (commit 5fb4be7) to hairmnl_js_error naming to avoid collision with Elevar's js_error. GTM trigger should have been updated at that time.
notes:
Handoff file: docs/gtm-js-error-handoff-2026-05-06.md

GTM fixes needed (live container v140, all HIGH priority):

1. GA4 - JS Error Event (tag 776): Change trigger from 'Initialization - All Pages' → 'Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error' (trigger 772). Keep eventName=js_error. Publish.

2. Conversion Linker (tag 12): Broken trigger 2147479553. Fix by reconnecting to valid trigger. Impact: all GA4 conversion attribution broken.

3. Klaviyo - Form Submission Listener (tag 415): Broken trigger 2147479553. Fix by reconnecting to valid trigger. Impact: email automation flows broken.

4. TrafficGuard Tag (tag 94): Broken trigger 2147479553. Fix by reconnecting to valid trigger. Impact: fraud detection disabled.

All 4 tags share the same non-existent trigger ID — likely a deleted custom event trigger. Fix all in same GTM session, then publish container.

Dashboard update: scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py line ~503 — change _eq('eventName', 'js_error') to _in('eventName', ['js_error', 'hairmnl_js_error']).

title: GitHub Action: run dashboard daily, commit artifacts, trigger Pages deploy

Requires three repo secrets added via GH UI: GA4_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY, PSI_API_KEY, GA4_PROPERTY_ID.

title: Impact-weighted Top friction pages ranking — sort by poor_count not poor%
description: Top friction pages currently sort by (-p_poor, -total) at line ~344, ranking a 100-pageview page at 50% poor (50 events) above a 1M-pageview page at 3% poor (30k events). Change sort key to (-(p_poor * total), -total) to prioritize impact. Surface the impact column (formatted poor_count) and N in render_top_pages() at line ~1028.

title: Forward-only bot filter in query_ga4_rum() — exclude /pages/privacy-policy
description: Per bd memory ga4-privacy-policy-bot-2026-05-01: /pages/privacy-policy receives ~538k bot events/14d (49x homepage volume), contaminating RUM page-level metrics and friction-page rankings. Add a GA4 dimensionFilter clause to exclude this URL from page-level queries (top friction pages, INP attribution by page). Forward-only — old snapshots remain unfiltered. Document the cutoff in dashboard/README.md.

title: Blog article hero image LCP — add preload, WebP picture, fetchpriority
description: GA4 RUM shows 40–88% poor LCP on hair-education blog articles (worst: flattering-haircuts-for-every-face-shape at 88.5%, n=61). Root cause: article hero image likely lacks fetchpriority=high, link rel=preload, and WebP picture element with srcset. Fix: audit sections/article.liquid for hero image handling, add all three optimizations, push to draft + live.

title: Lazy-init below-fold Swipers via IntersectionObserver to cut homepage INP
description: Homepage initializes 8 Swiper instances at DOMContentLoaded (7 .swiper.collection + 1 banner-slider). With 315 swiper-slides on the page, layout measurement + touch listener binding + initial positioning is serial main-thread work blocking the user's first interaction (97% of poor INPs are debug_target=(not set), i.e. TBT-bound). Strategy: keep above-fold (banner + first .swiper.collection) eager, defer remaining .swiper.collection instances until they enter a 200px-rootMargin IntersectionObserver. Implement as a wrap-and-override script in layout/theme.liquid so we don't touch the bundled custom-theme.js. Constraints: visual parity required, below-fold carousels must initialize before user scrolls to them, push to draft 140785582179 first.
notes:
2026-05-02 ship: assets/custom-theme.js around line 14352-14430.

PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED BY ua7 (b619fe2, 2026-04-29). When this issue was
filed today the bd notes on u1a still treated lazy-init as un-shipped,
but git log shows b619fe2 IS on main and shopify theme pull from live
131664707683 confirms IS LIVE. So the original framing of this issue
("introduce lazy-init") is already done.

INCREMENTAL CHANGE I SHIPPED:
ua7 had `index === 0 || top < innerHeight` to force-eager the first
.swiper.collection. On mobile that slider is below-fold (y~1500-2000px
on 320-414px viewport), so the safety net was wasted DCL work.

Edited assets/custom-theme.js:14411 — dropped `index === 0`, now pure
`top < innerHeight` viewport gate.

DEPLOYED:
- Draft theme 140785582179: pushed 2026-05-02, byte-verified via
  shopify theme pull → 739655 bytes match local exactly
- Live theme 131664707683: PENDING user confirmation

EXPECTED IMPACT: -30 to -80ms TBT at DCL on mobile homepage where the
first slider is below-fold. Stacks on ua7's already-shipped win.

VISUAL PARITY: above-fold sliders (rect.top < innerHeight) still eager;
below-fold use IO with 200px rootMargin so user never sees a blank
slider — behavior identical to ua7 except for the index===0 fallback
path. Banner-slider unaffected (separate eager init block at line 14330).
close_reason: Shipped: dropped index===0 forced-eager from collection-slider init. Pushed to live 131664707683 (commit 6e78a0f). All 8 below-fold sliders now IO-deferred on mobile homepage.

Pattern matches GDPR/privacy compliance scrapers. This contaminates 60-76% of aggregate CLS/LCP/FCP/TTFB RUM. Action: GA4 admin -> Data Streams -> Web -> Configure Tag Settings -> Internal Traffic OR add Filter to exclude pagePath=/pages/privacy-policy. Verification: re-run scripts/web-vitals-report.py --days 7 and confirm aggregate event totals drop 60%+.

Bot events (~538k/14d) will stop being collected from this page going forward. Historical contamination remains but future aggregate RUM will be clean.

title: Audit vendor.js for INP-blocking code — INP 280ms target ~200ms

Profile via Chrome DevTools Performance tab on first tap interaction; identify long tasks; split or defer where possible. Lower-stakes than CLS/LCP but moves a Core Web Vital that affects ranking.

All 8 below-fold .swiper.collection elements on homepage now IO-deferred. Expected -30 to -80ms TBT on mobile. Audit phase complete — INP improvement estimated but not yet CrUX-confirmed. Schedule CrUX validation 2026-05-27 (28-day rollup, GA4 property 248106289).

title: Add sizes attribute to product thumbnails — avoid mobile downloading 4100w variants
description: Product thumbnails in snippets/product-grid-item.liquid:140 lack a sizes attribute, so mobile browsers download desktop-size variants (up to 4100w) on collection pages. Add sizes='(min-width: 1024px) 25vw, (min-width: 640px) 33vw, 50vw' or similar matching the responsive grid. Expected: LCP -0.5s on mobile collection pages.
close_reason: Already shipped in earlier work — verified via grep on snippets/product-grid-item.liquid. The primary tags at lines 174, 202, 226 all have sizes='(min-width: 1024px) 25vw, (min-width: 640px) 33vw, 50vw'. Mobile correctly picks 360w/540w variants instead of 1080w+. Only the noscript fallback at line 242 lacks sizes — irrelevant since noscript only fires when JS is fully disabled. No new code needed; closing as already-done.

title: Fix CLS 0.30 — homepage hover-state width/height redraw on .double__image
description: Top single fix in Phase 2 perf priorities — CLS p75 0.30 is 3x the POOR threshold. Root cause: hover-state width/height redraw on .double__image:hover .collection__image__bottom (assets/theme.css:14679). Remove the redundant width/height from the hover state so hover does not trigger a layout-shifting redraw. Expected: CLS 0.30 → ~0.10.

Hover state at assets/theme.css:12222 no longer declares width/height — only opacity/visibility/transition. Inline comment documents the CLS fix (-0.05 lab) + INP improvement. Roadmap reference to line 14679 was stale (file shifted with subsequent CSS extractions). Field CLS p75 0.30 has additional contributors beyond this one hover redraw — investigate via GA4 web-vitals attribution data once 24-48h flows (filing follow-up issue).

(2) PDP widgets show 18+20=38 products; reduce to 6-8 per widget (Baymard/NN/g best practice). (3) PDP widgets stacked at 32-38% scroll depth; place FBT under Add-to-Cart, YMAL further down. (4) Homepage has 0 widgets; add Trending or Bestsellers. Same backend, free.
notes:

This closes Fix #2 from the original audit ("reduce to 6-8 per widget per Baymard/NN/g best practice"). 8 lands within the recommended 6-8 range.

Expected perf impact:

Verification (24-48h, real-shopper RUM dashboard):

- top_pages.LCP for PDPs — kerastase-blond-absolu and similar should see incremental LCP improvement (additive to z9y + ojx already shipped)

Remaining fixes from original audit (NOT done):

- Fix #3 — reposition PDP widgets (FBT under ATC; YMAL further down)

title: Add click-to-scroll wrapper to Judge.me static stars (PDP only)
description: Original Judge.me hydrated badge wraps stars in a clickable element bound to scroll to #judgeme_product_reviews. Branch's static stars have no click target — UX regression on PDP. Fix in snippets/judgeme_static_stars.liquid: accept optional scroll_target param, wrap in <a href> when set. Pass scroll_target='#judgeme_product_reviews' from judgeme_widgets.liquid only when called from PDP context (not collection cards).
close_reason: Implemented in commit 68f41fb. judgeme_static_stars.liquid accepts optional scroll_target param + conditionally wraps stars in <a>. judgeme_widgets.liquid passes '#judgeme_product_reviews' only when template == 'product'. Preserves Judge.me's original click-to-scroll UX on PDP including related-product carousels (matches loader.js behavior of binding same handler to all .jdgm-prev-badge on page). Pushed to draft theme 140785582179.

title: Verify Judge.me loader.js queries .jdgm-prev-badge vs .jdgm-preview-badge
description: Branch renames wrapper class from jdgm-preview-badge to jdgm-prev-badge. Open hairmnl.com/products/<any-product-with-reviews> in DevTools, find loader.js from cdn.judge.me, search source for both prev-badge and preview-badge. Whichever appears as querySelector argument is what loader hydrates. If loader queries the full word, click-to-scroll regression is permanent unless we add href wrapper. Blocks merge decision.
close_reason: Verified by fetching loader.js v490 from cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../judgeme-490/assets/loader.js. Loader maps widget files to gating selectors; widget/main.js (the badge hydration code) is gated on '.jdgm-preview-badge, .judgeme-preview-badge' (full word, both spellings). Branch's renamed '.jdgm-prev-badge' (short) wrappers will NOT match — widget/main.js never downloads on pages with only preview badges. Bonus: even on PDPs with review widgets where loader fires for other selectors, the badge hydration JS is skipped. No race, no flicker, no double-render. Click-to-scroll regression is permanent (loader handler bound to .jdgm-preview-badge can't find renamed wrappers) — exactly what 68f41fb proactively fixed via <a href> wrapper.

title: Port forloop-based eager loading from product-grid-item to product-grid-item-branded (~25 brand collection pages)
description:
Port the forloop-based 3-tier eager-loading pattern from snippets/product-grid-item.liquid to snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid. Identified by OC HH-SWARM 2026-05-18 c2k Phase B audit.

## The gap

snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid line 169 has `loading="lazy"` on ALL product cards unconditionally. No `forloop.first` check, no `loading_priority` param, no `fetchpriority` attribute.

snippets/product-grid-item.liquid (the default) implements a 3-tier pattern at lines 166-193:
- forloop.first → loading="eager" + fetchpriority="high"
- forloop.index 2-4 → loading="eager"
- forloop.index 5+ → loading="lazy"

## Affected pages (~25)

All branded landing pages that route through `product-grid-item-branded.liquid` via `{% include %}`:
- sections/collection-branded.liquid (generic branded)
- sections/collection-branded-subcollection.liquid
- sections/brand-collection.liquid
- 25+ branded-specific sections: collection-lorealprofessionnel, collection-krsymbiose, collection-lp-*, collection-kr*, collection-blondabsolu, etc.

Examples: /collections/kerastase-symbiose, /collections/loreal-professionnel, /collections/blond-absolu

## NOT affected (already fixed)

- /collections/products-for-rebonded-hair → uses default collection.liquid → product-grid-item.liquid (already has the fix)
- /collections/kerastase-heat-protectors → same as above

## Why include (not render) makes this simple

product-grid-item-branded.liquid is called via `{% include %}` which shares parent scope. `forloop.first` and `forloop.index` are accessible inside the snippet without parameter passing — no callsite changes required.
design:
## Files to change

- `snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid` (1 file, ~10-15 lines)

## The change

Replace line 169 `loading="lazy"` with a conditional pattern that mirrors the default snippet:

```liquid
{% if forloop.first %}
  loading="eager"
  fetchpriority="high"
{% elsif forloop.index <= 4 %}
  loading="eager"
{% else %}
  loading="lazy"
{% endif %}
```

Keep the existing WebP picture pattern (lines 151-158) and srcset (lines 161-166) as-is — no change to image dimensions/format logic.

## Pre-flight verification

1. `grep -n "include 'product-grid-item-branded'" sections/*.liquid` — confirm all callsites use include (not render).
2. `grep -n "forloop" snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid` — confirm no existing forloop assumptions conflict.
3. Diff against snippets/product-grid-item.liquid lines 166-193 for pattern parity.

## Scope/risk

- LOW risk — single snippet, additive conditionals, no callsite changes.
- Branded sections that have MULTIPLE forloops (e.g., best_sellers + new_arrivals carousels) will get eager loading on the first item of EACH carousel. This is acceptable — carousel first items are typically above-the-fold.
- Specifically: collection-branded.liquid line 598 uses product-grid-item-branded inside the <=6 best-sellers path; forloop.first there is the first best-seller (a reasonable LCP candidate).

## Multi-form theme push checklist (per CLAUDE.md)

1. Push to draft theme 140785582179 first: `shopify theme push --theme=140785582179 --only=snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid --nodelete`.
2. Smoke test: open /collections/kerastase-symbiose in incognito DevTools. Confirm first product card img has `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"`, cards 2-4 have `loading="eager"`, cards 5+ have `loading="lazy"`.
3. After smoke pass, push to live 131664707683 with `--allow-live`.
4. Stage + commit + push to remote with reference to this bd id.

## Out of scope (file as separate bd)

- The preconnect/dns-prefetch upgrade finding from c2k Phase C (separate ticket).
- Article hero LCP optimizations (none needed per c2k Phase C audit — `.fade-in-child` already inert).
acceptance_criteria:
1. snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid has the 3-tier loading pattern matching the default snippet.
2. View-source on /collections/kerastase-symbiose (or any branded landing): first product card img has `loading="eager"` AND `fetchpriority="high"`.
3. View-source: card #2-4 imgs have `loading="eager"`, card #5+ imgs have `loading="lazy"`.
4. No regressions: existing WebP picture pattern + srcset breakpoints unchanged.
5. PSI mobile run on /collections/kerastase-symbiose shows LCP element is now eager-loaded (no late LCP from lazy hero).
6. Pushed to live theme 131664707683, commit referenced in bd notes, branch up to date with origin/main.
notes:
Dispatched to OC lead-architect (PAID tier — deepseek-v4-pro:cloud) via canonical /offload → /hh-orchestrate. CC stays as merge-gate.

## MERGE_GATE_PASS 2026-05-18

OC dispatch + CC merge-gate complete.

### Push timeline
- Draft push (140785582179): commit (pre-stage), file content verified via shopify theme pull
- Live push (131664707683): commit landed; CDN propagation ~20s
- Cache-busted live smoke on /collections/kerastase-symbiose: 3-tier pattern rendering correctly

### Live smoke result (post-CDN-propagation)
- Card #1 (first branded grid card): loading=eager + fetchpriority=high ✓
- Cards 2-4: loading=eager ✓
- Cards 5+: loading=lazy ✓

### git show HEAD --stat
commit 89a304ea7a1507ad3f1add851be1c7b3b6fe31b2

Date:   Mon May 18 10:10:11 2026 +0200

    feat(product-grid-item-branded): port 3-tier eager-loading forloop pattern (bd hairmnl-theme-987.1)
    
    Replace unconditional loading="lazy" at line 169 with the same 3-tier
    pattern that snippets/product-grid-item.liquid uses for the default
    collection grid:

title: P4.3: Lighthouse CI workflow — fail PR on perf regression
description:
Goal: add a GitHub Action that runs Lighthouse against PR previews and fails the PR if PSI drops > 5 points or CLS > 0.05 on any of the 5 template URLs. Prevents the next perf regression.

## Steps

1. Add `.github/workflows/lighthouse-ci.yml`:
   - On `pull_request`, install lighthouse-cli
   - Run lighthouse against the dev preview URL for the 5 template paths
   - Compare against a `lighthouse-baselines.json` file checked into the repo
   - Fail the workflow if PSI drops > 5 points OR CLS increases > 0.05 on any URL
2. Add `lighthouse-baselines.json` to the repo root, with the median values from P4.1.
3. Update `lighthouse-baselines.json` whenever an intentional perf-affecting change merges (rare).

## Constraints

- Lighthouse-CI needs a way to authenticate against the dev preview. Either:
  - Use the public preview URL (with `?preview_theme_id=141168312419`) — works if the dev theme stays unpublished
  - Use a `_password` cookie if the dev theme has password protection
- GitHub Actions runners are not the same hardware as PSI's runners; relative comparisons are valid but absolute scores will differ.

## Acceptance

- Workflow file committed
- Baselines file committed with P4.1 medians
- Workflow runs successfully on at least one PR
- Failure path tested: deliberately introduce a perf regression in a test PR, verify workflow fails

## Out of scope

- Replacing PSI as the authoritative measurement (PSI is still authoritative for releases)
- A staging URL or non-production preview (use the dev theme preview URL)

title: P4.2: CrUX field-data monitoring methodology — post-cutover plan
description:
Goal: write a methodology doc for monitoring CrUX (field) data post-cutover. We cannot measure field data on the dev theme; this is the plan to monitor it after publishing.

## Methodology

CrUX (Chrome User Experience Report) publishes 28-day rolling p75 LCP / CLS / INP for any URL Chrome users have visited. Available via:
- CrUX API (https://chromeuxreport.googleapis.com/v1/records:queryRecord) — programmatic
- PageSpeed Insights (the "Field Data" section in PSI results) — same data, human readable
- Search Console "Core Web Vitals" report — Google-curated, lags by ~16 days

## Steps

1. Document the post-cutover monitoring cadence:
   - Day 0 (cutover): record lab + initial field (which will be P6-mix)
   - Week 1: record field (still mostly P6-mix in 28-day window)
   - Week 2: record (P8 contribution growing)
   - Week 4: record (~50% P8 in 28-day window)
   - Week 8: record (~100% P8) — this is the "real" comparison
2. Specify which URLs to monitor (the same 5 templates from P0.1).
3. Set up a recurring task (Beads cron or manual checklist) to capture these readings.
4. Write the doc as `os2-migration/post-cutover-monitoring.md`.

## Acceptance

- `os2-migration/post-cutover-monitoring.md` committed
- Monitoring cadence specified
- URL list specified
- One sample CrUX API request curl command included for reference

## Out of scope

- Actually running the monitoring (post-cutover work, not this epic)
- Setting up alerting / dashboards (separate epic if needed)

title: P3.6: Cart drawer reload via section.shopify.refresh (OS 2.0-native)
description:
Goal: replace the current cart drawer reload pattern (which fetches `?section_id=api-cart-items` and innerHTML-splices the response) with the OS 2.0-native `section.shopify.refresh` API. More efficient, handles section JS bindings automatically.

## Current pattern (P8 stock)

```js
// In theme.js loadForm():
window.fetch(window.theme.routes.root_url + "?section_id=api-cart-items").then(...).then(t => {
    // innerHTML splice into this.items
});
```

## Target pattern

```js
Shopify.section.refresh('api-cart-items');
// or for a specific section ID:
Shopify.section.refresh('drawer-cart-section-id');
```

The `Shopify.section.refresh` API:
- Fetches the section via the same mechanism
- Replaces the section in the DOM
- Re-binds any section-level event handlers (which manual innerHTML doesn't)
- Triggers `shopify:section:load` event for any listeners

## Constraints

- This is a change to P8's stock cart drawer reload logic. We MUST NOT modify `assets/theme.js` (prohibited file).
- Instead, intercept the cart drawer's reload flow from `hairmnl-custom.js` (or its split successor):
  - Listen for `theme:cart:reload` event
  - Call `e.stopImmediatePropagation()` to suppress P8's stock handler
  - Call `Shopify.section.refresh('api-cart-items')` instead

## Acceptance

- Cart drawer content reload uses `Shopify.section.refresh`, not manual innerHTML
- Cart drawer still opens and shows the newly-added item on ATC (tonight's v2 fix preserved)
- Section JS bindings (quantity steppers, remove buttons) work after reload
- No regression on cart drawer behaviour

## Out of scope

- /cart page (standalone) — this is for the drawer only
- BSS B2B cart customizations (separate)

title: P3.5: fetchpriority + preload for LCP element + critical fonts
description: Goal: prioritize the LCP element + critical font using OS 2.0 / modern HTML attributes. ## Steps 1. **Identify the LCP element per template**: - / (home): likely the hero image in slideshow.liquid - /collections/best-sellers: likely the page title H1 OR the first product image - /products/: likely the main product image gallery - /cart: likely the page title - /collections/loreal-professionnel: likely the brand featured image 2. **Add `fetchpriority="high"`** to the LCP `` element. For Liquid image renders, pass `fetchpriority: 'high'`. 3. **Add ``** for: - LCP image (only on templates where it's known — use `if template contains 'index'` etc.) - `BasisGrotesquePro-Regular.woff2` (critical font for body text) - `SelfModern-Regular.woff2` (critical font for headings) 4. **Defer remaining fonts** with `` for primary, then `font-display: optional` on non-critical weights. ## Files - layout/theme.liquid (preload links in ``) - sections/slideshow.liquid or snippet-image.liquid (fetchpriority attribute) ## Acceptance - LCP element on homepage has `fetchpriority="high"` attribute - 2 critical fonts have `` in `` - Mobile LCP improves by ≥ 0.5s (lab, n=3) - Desktop LCP improves by ≥ 0.2s (lab, n=3) ## Out of scope - Identifying LCP on every possible template (cover the 5 acceptance-criteria templates only) - Image format work (WebP already shipped)

title: P3.4: Lazy-render heavy below-fold sections via Section API + IO
description:
Goal: defer the entire render of heavy below-fold sections to client-side fetch via Section Rendering API + IntersectionObserver. Trade slightly slower below-fold first-paint for substantially faster TTI on mobile.

## Sections to lazy-render

- Home: brand-grid (loads all 30+ brand logos)
- Home: featured-collections (3-row × N-col product grid)
- PDP: related-products / Buy It With

## Approach

1. In server-rendered Liquid, render only a `<div class="section-placeholder" data-section-url="..." data-section-id="...">` placeholder where the heavy section would normally go.
2. Add JS (in hairmnl-common.js) that uses IntersectionObserver:
   - When placeholder enters 200px before viewport, fetch its section
   - Replace the placeholder with the response HTML
   - Initialize any section-specific JS (e.g., Flickity sliders inside the section)
3. For SEO, ensure server-rendered Liquid still includes the section's text content somewhere reachable (e.g., in a `<noscript>` block) so crawlers see it.

## Constraints

- Don't lazy-render the LCP element. Confirm the LCP is the hero image, NOT a below-fold section.
- Don't break filter / sort URL params (P3.2 functionality).

## Acceptance

- 2 below-fold heavy sections lazy-render
- Page weight (transferred bytes) drops by ≥ 200 KB on / (homepage)
- TBT drops by ≥ 150ms on / (homepage)
- SEO: `view-source` for / still contains the section text content via `<noscript>`

## Out of scope

- All sections lazy-rendered (too aggressive — pick 2-3 high-value targets)
- Filter / sort interaction with lazy-rendered sections (out of scope for first iteration)

title: P3.3: content-visibility:auto on below-fold sections (5 templates)
description:
Goal: apply `content-visibility: auto` to below-the-fold sections. Browser skips render / layout / paint for off-screen content, then renders on scroll-near. Pair with `contain-intrinsic-size` to reserve the correct space (preventing scrollbar jumps).

## Browser support

`content-visibility: auto` is Chrome 85+, Edge 85+, Firefox 119+, Safari 18+. Coverage > 90%. Older browsers ignore the property (no harm).

## Steps

1. Identify below-fold sections per template:
   - Home: best-sellers row, featured collections, brand grid (slider C), category tile grid
   - Collection: pagination + sub-grids
   - PDP: Buy It With row, related products, description tabs
   - Brand-collection: best_sellers, new_arrivals, left-media-with-text, right-image-with-text

2. Add to `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` (new section):
   ```css
   /* P3.3: skip rendering off-screen sections */
   .home-best-sellers,
   .home-featured-collections,
   .home-brand-grid,
   .pdp-buy-it-with,
   .pdp-description-tabs {
     content-visibility: auto;
     contain-intrinsic-size: auto 800px;  /* tune per section */
   }
   ```

3. Tune `contain-intrinsic-size` per section so the placeholder height matches the rendered height (prevents scrollbar jump as user scrolls in).

## kt0 caveat

`contain-intrinsic-size` doesn't trigger kt0 (no `contain: layout/strict/etc.` and no `transform`). But verify with `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` to be safe.

## Acceptance

- ≥ 5 below-fold sections have `content-visibility: auto` applied
- No visible scrollbar jump as user scrolls down on the affected pages
- Lighthouse "Avoid an excessive DOM size" or "Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers" improves OR doesn't regress
- kt0 lint clean

## Out of scope

- Sections that contain fixed-position descendants (kt0 rule — avoid this combo)
- Above-fold sections

title: P3.2: Section Rendering API for collection filter/sort (no page reload)
description:
Goal: replace full-page reloads on collection filter / sort changes with Section Rendering API fetches (`?section_id=...`) that only re-render the product grid.

## P6 behaviour (current)

Filter change → form submit → full page reload (~3-5s on mobile Slow 4G).

## P8 native behaviour (target)

Filter change → `fetch(currentURL + '?section_id=collection-grid')` → response is the freshly-rendered section HTML → swap into the page's grid container → no page reload.

## Steps

1. Identify the filter / sort form in `sections/brand-collection.liquid` AND any standard collection section (likely `sections/collection-template.liquid` or similar).
2. Add a JS handler that:
   - Intercepts form submission
   - Builds the request URL with all current filter params
   - Appends `?section_id=<section-name>` (where section-name is the collection grid section)
   - Fetches that URL, gets the response text (HTML)
   - Replaces the existing grid container's innerHTML with the response
   - Updates browser URL via `history.pushState`
3. Wire to all filter checkboxes, sort selects, and the price-range slider.
4. Don't fetch on every keystroke for text inputs — debounce 300ms.

## Files

- assets/hairmnl-collection.js (new file from P2.5 split, or directly in hairmnl-common.js)
- sections/brand-collection.liquid (might need data attributes for JS hook)

## Acceptance

- Filter / sort change on /collections/best-sellers does NOT reload the page
- Grid updates with the filtered products
- URL bar reflects the new filter state (history.pushState)
- Back button restores prior filter state
- Mobile + desktop both functional

## Out of scope

- Pagination (separate ticket if also full-reload)
- Search results page (different template; same pattern though)

title: P2.5: Tree-shake hairmnl-custom.js — per-template bundle split
description:
Goal: split `assets/hairmnl-custom.js` (~370 LoC, 7 sections) into per-template bundles loaded conditionally, so collection/PDP/cart pages don't pay JS-parse cost for unused behaviours.

## Sections + their template scope

1. `sticky-filter-bar` — collection only
2. `product-tabs-v1` — PDP only
3. `header-mobile-slide-rule` — every template
4. `lion-points` — every template (but only if user is logged in)
5. `domparser` — every template
6. `hover-line` — every template (desktop nav)
7. `cart-drawer-on-atc` — every template

## Proposed split

- `hairmnl-common.js`: sections 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 (~250 LoC) — loaded on every template
- `hairmnl-collection.js`: section 1 (~30 LoC) — loaded if `{% if template contains 'collection' %}`
- `hairmnl-product.js`: section 2 (~30 LoC) — loaded if `{% if template contains 'product' %}`

## Steps

1. Split `hairmnl-custom.js` into three files in `assets/`.
2. Update `layout/theme.liquid` to load `hairmnl-common.js` unconditionally, and the others via Liquid template gating.
3. Each file remains a single IIFE for the script's outer scope.
4. Verify each section still works on its target templates.

## Acceptance

- Three files (`hairmnl-common.js`, `hairmnl-collection.js`, `hairmnl-product.js`) replace `hairmnl-custom.js`
- Conditional loads in `layout/theme.liquid` use `{% if template contains '...' %}` guards
- Each behaviour verified working on its target template (sticky filter bar on /collections/best-sellers, tabs on /products/<top>, etc.)

## Out of scope

- Combining with `theme.js` (prohibited — would mean editing P8's stock bundle)

title: Fix homepage slider gray-placeholder (A+B: CSS height cap + IO rootMargin bump)
description: See design field. Two-edit fix for ~1185px gray blocks appearing on below-fold homepage collection sliders for several seconds. Already implemented in assets/custom-theme.css + assets/custom-theme.js this session. Awaiting draft-theme push + visual verification.
design:
# Homepage slider gray-placeholder fix (A + B)

## Problem (diagnosed 2026-05-16)
User reports: huge gray placeholders appear on some homepage collection-slider sections below the fold, visible for several seconds before products materialize.

Verified via live DOM inspection on www.hairmnl.com:
- The 1st slider on the page initialized (height=341px, no spinner)
- Sliders 2-6 below the fold were still in pre-init state with `spinner-wrapper` height = **1185px each** (3.5× the post-init height)
- The 1185px is because `.swiper-wrapper` is a flex row of slides at width:100%/height:100% — pre-init, all slides stack and wrapper stretches to the tallest content height
- Spinner-wrapper at `position:absolute; height:100%; background:#e0e0e0` covers all of that

## Why "some but not all"
Sliders use lazy IntersectionObserver init with `rootMargin: 200px 0px`. Because each pre-init slider is 1185px tall (wider than viewport+margin), only one slider fits in the IO window at a time → they initialize **sequentially as user scrolls past each one's top**, never together. Combined with main-thread contention from 9+ third-party widgets, each slider lags 1-3 seconds after its IO callback fires.

## Fix
**A (CSS):** Cap pre-init slider height via `.collection-slider .swiper.collection:not(.swiper-initialized) { max-height: 500px; overflow: hidden; }`. Real post-init height is 341px desktop / 420px mobile, so 500px is comfortably above without showing the giant pre-init stretch. Cap lifts when Swiper sets `.swiper-initialized` class.

**B (JS):** Bump IntersectionObserver `rootMargin` from `200px 0px` → `1200px 0px`. With Fix A capping pre-init slider height to 500px, several sliders now fit in the IO trigger zone and batch-init together.

Combined effect: gray placeholder visible <100ms (was 1-3s), at correct 500px height (was 1185px), and below-fold sliders init proactively instead of reactively.

## Files changed
- `assets/custom-theme.css` — +12 lines (CSS rule + comment)
- `assets/custom-theme.js` — +9 lines (1 value change + 8 lines of comment)

## Verification plan
1. Push to draft theme 140785582179 (`shopify theme push --theme=140785582179 --only=assets/custom-theme.css --only=assets/custom-theme.js --nodelete`)
2. Open draft preview in Chrome incognito, hard reload, scroll homepage. Confirm gray placeholders are <500px tall and resolve quickly.
3. Network throttling test (Fast 3G): confirm gray box still proportional even under slow main thread.
4. Once verified, push to live with `--allow-live`.
5. Re-run live DOM inspection: `slider.previousElementSibling.getBoundingClientRect().height` should be ≤500 for pre-init sliders.
acceptance_criteria: Fix pushed to draft theme. Visual verification on draft: gray placeholder ≤500px tall, resolves within ~500ms after slider scrolls into 1200px IO window. Live push with --allow-live. Post-live DOM check: pre-init slider heights ≤500px. Commit message references this bd ID.
notes:
Implemented + pushed live 2026-05-16. Verified via DOM inspection on live www.hairmnl.com:
- All 7 .swiper.collection elements show pre-init height = 500px (was 1185px in pre-fix measurement). 58% reduction in gray-placeholder visual jank.
- 0/7 initialized at 5s post-load with no scroll (sliders below viewport+1200px-margin); expected per IO design. Init triggers on scroll.
- Diff: assets/custom-theme.css +12 lines, assets/custom-theme.js +9 lines (1 value change + 8 line comment block). git show HEAD --stat to be appended after commit.
Commit landed on main:
commit 1cd6ae498d4cba4e714954061f9c844f91dc36ef
Author: Jonathan Joson <jjoson@clickcommerce.digital>
Date:   Sat May 16 23:07:35 2026 +0200

Closing as verified-on-live.

title: P3.2: App deprecation monitoring (vendor changelogs)
description: For the top 6 apps from P1.1 (highest dependency: Klaviyo, Reamaze, LimeSpot, Elevar, Pro Blogger, Octane AI — adjust based on matrix), set up monitoring of their changelogs / release notes. Either: subscribe to vendor email lists with a dedicated alias, OR write a small scraper, OR add quarterly manual-check reminders. Goal: get advance notice when a vendor announces TAE-only future.
design:
# Design notes — dispatch prep (2026-05-17)

## Output path (explicit, do not deviate)

The single output file is **`os2-migration/vendor-monitoring.md`** — top-level `os2-migration/`, **NOT** `docs/os2-migration/`. The directory already exists with README.md, feasibility.html, app-compatibility-matrix.md, customization-audit.md, perf-baseline-comparison.md. Place the new file there. No `docs/` prefix.

## Input: which 6 apps to monitor

Read `os2-migration/app-compatibility-matrix.md` (closed bd hairmnl-theme-80z0.1) and pick the 6 apps with the highest combined "current dependency × deprecation risk." Suggested starting set if not adjusting:

1. Klaviyo (email/SMS — heavy on-page weight, snippet integration)
2. Re:amaze (live chat — 213 KB, hardcoded snippet)
3. LimeSpot / Personalizer.io (recommendations — 249 KB always-on)
4. Elevar (conversion tracking — 2.6s PSI latency reported)
5. Pro Blogger / SA (multiple legacy snippets, no TAE)
6. Searchanise (hardcoded script tag, NEED-VERIFY for TAE availability)

If you swap a different app into the top-6 based on the matrix, justify the swap in the doc.

## Approach to decide between

Per the ticket: pick ONE of the three (or a hybrid) and document the choice:

1. **Email-alias subscription** — set up a shared inbox (e.g., `migration-alerts@hairmnl.com`) and subscribe to each vendor's "product updates" / "release notes" / "developer changelog" newsletter. Pros: zero maintenance. Cons: vendors may not have a list.
2. **Scraper** — small Python or Node script that polls each vendor's changelog page weekly and diffs. Pros: catches changes even without email. Cons: maintenance burden when vendors restructure their docs.
3. **Quarterly manual check** — calendar reminder; one person spends 30 min per quarter scanning vendor changelogs. Pros: simple, reliable. Cons: lag time up to 90 days.

Hybrid is acceptable — e.g., email-alias for vendors who have lists + quarterly manual for those who don't.

## Doc structure (suggested)

1. **Approach chosen** — one paragraph justifying the pick.
2. **Per-app monitoring table** — for each of the 6 apps: app name, vendor changelog URL (or "n/a"), email-list signup URL (or "n/a"), monitoring mechanism, who's on the hook.
3. **First quarterly review** — calendar date (90 days out from doc-landing date, so 2026-08-15-ish).
4. **Escalation criteria** — what counts as a "TAE-only announcement" that fires migration trigger #1 (per `os2-migration/feasibility.html` triggers section).
5. **Maintenance** — how this doc gets updated when vendors change their changelog setup.

## Scope cap

≤120 LoC for the doc. If a scraper is the chosen approach, ≤80 lines of code in `scripts/vendor-changelog-watch.py` (or similar) — that's the second file. Keep total under 2 files / 200 LoC across the dispatch.

## What NOT to do

- Do not write the actual scraper / monitoring infra if email-alias or manual is the chosen approach. The decision doc is the deliverable; infra is a follow-up if needed.
- Do not implement the monitoring (subscribing emails, creating calendar invites, etc.) — those require credentials and human action. Document the plan.
- Do not touch any file outside `os2-migration/` or (if scraper chosen) `scripts/`.
- Do not create intermediate working-notes files (`raw-data.md`, `working-notes.md`, etc.) — the QA pass on P1.2 caught this and it produces clean-up debt.

## Phantom-completion guard

Before marking close in your bd notes, paste the output of `git show HEAD --stat` to prove the commit landed with the expected file(s).
acceptance_criteria: Monitoring approach decided + documented. Each of the 6 apps has a 'how we'll find out about deprecation' mechanism. First quarterly review scheduled.

title: Preload mismatch on article pages — wasted preload
description: OC's Phase 1 audit for epic 01vl (commit b6ptavvez) discovered that on at least 1 article page (/blogs/hair-education/salon-hair-color-vs-box-dye-which-one-is-best-for-you), the generated in layout/theme.liquid references articles/78.png but the actual hero is articles/47.png. Preload is wasted — the LCP element loads without preload benefit. This is the hero preload added by commit 9hw (LCP fix for hair-education articles). Logic must be picking up an article image other than article.image (probably article.image_url for a different field, OR cached state). Scope: layout/theme.liquid preload generation logic + verification on top 10 article pages. Likely 1 file, ~10 LoC fix once root cause is identified. Independent of the Pro Blogger removal scope (epic 01vl). File separately so it can ship as a fast standalone fix even before Pro Blogger removal lands.
notes: SHIPPED 2026-05-16 commit e639ee9 / pushed d1ac2d9. Root cause: not actually a 'preload mismatch' as OC initially framed. The real issue is that section.settings.blog_show_featured_image = false on top hair-education articles, so the hero block at sections/article.liquid:27-39 doesn't render. article.image preload (9hw, 2026-05-04) fires but the previewed image isn't shown — wasted preload. The actual LCP is the first body , marked fetchpriority+eager by article-content-with-img-dims (7tq, 2026-05-10) but never preloaded. Fix: layout/theme.liquid adds a second preload extracting the first from article.content via Liquid split chains. Browser handles double-preload safely. Verified: purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines now shows BOTH preloads (article.image AND first body image). Other top articles are on Shopify CDN-cached HTML; will pick up the fix after ~5-15 min expiry. title: Stky replacement: sticky bar CSS + kt0 lint guard update
description:
Add the sticky bar CSS and update the kt0 lint to cover the new overlay selector.

MODIFIED FILES:
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid — append a new dated block with .sticky-atc-bar styles
- scripts/check-overlay-css.py — add `.sticky-atc-bar` and `.sticky-atc-bar.is-visible` to OVERLAY_PATTERNS

CSS REQUIREMENTS:
- .sticky-atc-bar: position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 100; background: var(--color-body-bg); border-top: 1px solid var(--color-border); padding: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0) using fallback.
- Hidden state via [hidden] attribute (display: none).
- .is-visible class triggers transition: transform 200ms ease.
- Mobile breakpoint (≤768px): stack the layout vertically OR truncate the product title to a single line.
- Brand colors: reuse existing CSS tokens (--color-primary, --color-body-text, etc.).
- KT0-safe: NO contain/transform/filter/backdrop-filter/perspective/will-change on .sticky-atc-bar or its ancestors. The whole point of the lint update is to enforce this.

OWNER: OC via /offload.

SCOPE CAP: 2 file modifications, ≤80 LoC delta total.

DEPENDS ON: T1 (snippet defines the .sticky-atc-bar class).
design:
Reference the existing dated-block pattern in snippets/css-overrides.liquid — start the new block with a comment like:

/* 2026-05-16 — Sticky ATC bar (replaces Stky app) */

For mobile-safe-area: use env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for iOS notch devices.

OVERLAY_PATTERNS update in scripts/check-overlay-css.py — find the existing list (look for entries like ".notify-tab", ".modal--notify", ".cart-drawer", etc.) and append both `.sticky-atc-bar` and `.sticky-atc-bar.is-visible`.

Test the lint locally after the edit: `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` should exit 0 with no new violations.

Anti-pattern: do NOT use `transform: translateY(...)` on .sticky-atc-bar for the entrance transition. That creates a containing-block trap. Use `transition: opacity` or `transition: bottom` instead.

- On the draft theme, sticky bar appears with brand styling.
- iOS safe-area-inset-bottom respected (test on mobile preview).
- No transform/contain/filter on .sticky-atc-bar or .sticky-atc-bar's ancestors.

title: Stky replacement: assets/sticky-atc.dev.js + .js
description:
Create the JS module that drives the sticky add-to-cart bar.

NEW FILES:
- assets/sticky-atc.dev.js (readable source, ~150 LoC)
- assets/sticky-atc.js (minified mirror — dev↔min parity per house rule)

CONTRACT:
- Initialize on DOMContentLoaded.
- Use IntersectionObserver to watch the primary product form's ATC button (`[data-product-form] [type="submit"]` or equivalent).
- When the primary ATC scrolls out of view, remove `hidden` from the sticky bar and add `.is-visible` class for transition. Reverse when scrolled back into view.
- Wire the sticky bar's variant selector to the primary form's variant select via 'change' event listeners — single source of truth is the primary form.
- Sticky bar's Add to Cart submit should re-use the primary form's submission flow (call its submit handler or AJAX submit to /cart/add with the same payload).
- On successful add, trigger the existing cart drawer open event (whatever event the theme's cart drawer listens to).
- Fire a GA4 `add_to_cart` event from the sticky bar submit path (match the data shape of the existing primary-form event).
- Idempotent: safe to call init() twice (no double event listeners).

API KEY: none (no backend integration).

OWNER: OC via /offload.

SCOPE CAP: 2 files (dev + min), ≤200 LoC total in dev.js. If exceeded, decompose into T2a (IntersectionObserver + visibility) and T2b (variant sync + submit handler).

DEPENDS ON: T1.
design:
Pattern from any existing theme JS that uses IntersectionObserver. Check assets/theme.dev.js for the lazy-load and announcement-bar patterns.

State management:
- Module-level state: { observer, stickyEl, primaryFormEl, isVisible }
- No global window pollution beyond a single window.HairMNLStickyATC namespace if needed for debugging.

Edge cases to handle:
- Variant becomes sold-out → disable sticky bar ATC button (mirror primary form's disabled state)
- Page navigation (Shopify section rendering API) → re-initialize on section reload events
- Primary form not found (non-PDP) → bail silently
- IntersectionObserver unsupported (very old browsers) → polyfill or feature-detect and bail

Minification: use terser or simple manual minification. The dev.js and js files must produce IDENTICAL behavior — verify by manual diff of variable renames only.

Anti-pattern: do not duplicate the variant change logic. Use event delegation to the primary form's variant select.

Read-the-docs: not needed (browser APIs are stable).
acceptance_criteria:
- assets/sticky-atc.dev.js + assets/sticky-atc.js both exist.
- dev.js is ≤200 LoC.
- min.js is functionally identical to dev.js (manual diff: only whitespace + variable renames).
- On a sold-out PDP, sticky bar appears when scrolled past primary ATC.
- On an in-stock PDP, sticky bar appears with correct variant + price; ATC submit adds to cart and opens cart drawer.
- Variant change in primary form propagates to sticky bar (and vice versa).
- GA4 add_to_cart event fires once per successful add (verified in browser DevTools).
- No console errors on load or during scroll.
- IntersectionObserver feature-detect bails cleanly on unsupported browsers.

- Use Liquid `{{ product.featured_image | image_url: ... }}` and `{{ current_variant }}` from page context. - Hidden by default via `hidden` attribute; JS module toggles visibility. - Render-once: included via `{% render 'sticky-add-to-cart' %}` from layout/theme.liquid before . PATTERN: Mirror the structure used by snippets/product-form.liquid for variant + qty + ATC inputs. Reuse existing form action (/cart/add) and CSRF tokens. OWNER: OC via /offload. SCOPE CAP: 1 new file, ≤120 LoC. If exceeded, decompose before dispatching. DEPENDS ON: T0 (deck approved by stakeholders).
design:
Use the existing brand button styling and form CSRF tokens. Reference snippets/product-form.liquid lines around the {% form 'product' %} block.

The snippet must work both on PDPs (where product context is available) AND fail gracefully on non-PDP pages (where it should not render — guard with `{% if template contains 'product' %}` or render the empty shell and let JS handle product detection).

Variant selector should mirror the primary form's variant dropdown by ID/name so the JS module can sync state via event listeners.

For accessibility: bar must have role="region" aria-label="Sticky add to cart" and the qty input must have an associated <label>.

Read-the-docs: not needed (Shopify Liquid syntax stable).
acceptance_criteria:
- snippets/sticky-add-to-cart.liquid exists.
- File is ≤120 LoC.
- Renders empty/hidden when included in layout/theme.liquid before </body>.
- Markup parses cleanly in Shopify Theme Inspector.
- Variant selector reflects current_variant state.
- ATC button submits to /cart/add (verified via theme preview).
- Mobile layout doesn't overflow viewport horizontally at 375px.

title: BIS: SMS bridge serverless function (conditional on T0 decision)
description:
CONDITIONAL ticket. Only created/worked if T0's deck stakeholder review approves Option B (Semaphore, PH-local SMS) or Option C (Twilio).

If Option A (Skip SMS) is chosen: close this ticket as won't-fix.

WORK (if approved):
Build a minimal serverless function that receives a Klaviyo webhook on the BIS-flow fired event, formats the SMS body, and dispatches via the chosen provider.

LOCATION:
- Option B (Semaphore): new file in hairmnl-automation/scripts/bis-sms-bridge/index.js (serverless, deploy via Vercel or Cloudflare Workers — choose during ticket) or a new repo if hairmnl-automation isn't a great fit.
- Option C (Twilio): same shape, different provider.

INPUTS:
- Klaviyo webhook payload (verify signature using Klaviyo's webhook secret)
- Customer phone (E.164, "+63XXXXXXXXXX")
- Rendered SMS body (Klaviyo formats it; we just pass through)

OUTPUTS:
- Provider API call (Semaphore: POST https://api.semaphore.co/api/v4/messages; Twilio: POST https://api.twilio.com/.../Messages.json)
- Log row to JSONL or stdout (timestamp, recipient, provider message ID, cost in PHP)

OWNER: OC via /offload.

DEPENDS ON: T0 (decision), T1 (Klaviyo flow must have a webhook step).
design:
Stack TBD per deck decision. Recommended: Cloudflare Worker (low cold-start, generous free tier, easy secrets via wrangler.toml).

Authentication: Klaviyo signs webhooks with an HMAC-SHA256 hash of the body using a shared secret. Verify before processing.

Provider client: Semaphore has a simple REST API (just an api_key + number + message); Twilio uses basic auth (Account SID + Auth Token). Use the official Twilio Node SDK or fetch directly for Semaphore.

Cost tracking: write each send to a JSONL file in a Cloudflare R2 bucket or post a message to a Slack channel (configurable). hairmnl-data can later pull this for the analytics view (T8b extension).

Use context7 to fetch the chosen provider's latest docs before writing the call. Read-the-docs rule applies.
acceptance_criteria:
- Function deployed and reachable from Klaviyo webhook step.
- Klaviyo webhook signature verified on every request; invalid signatures rejected.
- Test SMS sent successfully to a real PH phone via the chosen provider.
- Cost-per-message logged.
- Klaviyo flow step pointed at the function URL.
- Documented secrets (env var names, where stored).

title: BIS: investigate Appikon CSV export + migration script
description:
Investigate whether Appikon offers a CSV export of in-flight back-in-stock subscriptions, and if so, write a one-shot import script to bring them into Klaviyo.

STEPS:
1. Email Appikon support: request CSV export of all pending BIS subscriptions (email, phone, variant ID, subscribe date). Subject line and body draft handled by CC.
2. If export is provided within 3 business days:
   - Write scripts/migrate_appikon_to_klaviyo.py — reads CSV, POSTs each row to Klaviyo BIS API using the server-side (private) Klaviyo key.
   - Idempotent: skip if email+variant already subscribed.
   - Dry-run mode first; actual import after sample-of-10 verification.
3. If no export or unresponsive:
   - Document the cutover risk: in-flight subscribers may silently miss their restock alert.
   - Mitigation A: leave Appikon flow in monitor-only mode for 30 days post-cutover (no theme code path; just don't uninstall yet).
   - Mitigation B: accept silent miss, add a banner to PDPs apologizing for the transition gap (T7 follow-up).

OWNER: Human (HairMNL ops, contacts Appikon) + CC (drafts email, writes script if needed).

DEPENDS ON: T1 (Klaviyo BIS API access needed to write import script).
BLOCKS: T7 (cutover decision rests on this).
design:
Email draft: friendly, business-tone, request: "We are migrating from Appikon to a Klaviyo-native back-in-stock implementation as part of our 2026 platform consolidation. Could you provide a CSV export of all currently pending back-in-stock subscriptions (customer email or phone, variant SKU or product handle, subscription timestamp) before we uninstall the app? This would help us avoid silently dropping in-flight subscribers." CC: jessica.joson@hairmnl.com.

If Appikon offers self-serve export in the app admin, prefer that path over support email.

Import script (if needed):
- Uses Klaviyo's server-side Profiles + BIS Subscription APIs (private key, set in env)
- Logs every row outcome (subscribed | already-existed | error) to a JSONL file
- Resumable: track high-watermark row index in a checkpoint file
acceptance_criteria:
- Appikon support contacted (email logged to bd notes).
- Either: CSV obtained AND import script run successfully (sample-of-10 verified, then full run), OR documented decision to accept cutover gap with explicit mitigation chosen.
- T7 (cutover) is unblocked.

title: BIS: add new overlay selectors to OVERLAY_PATTERNS (kt0 lint)
description:
Add the new overlay selectors introduced by T2 + T3 to scripts/check-overlay-css.py's OVERLAY_PATTERNS list, so the kt0 lint catches any future containing-block regressions on the new overlay.

NEW SELECTORS:
- .notify-tab (sticky right-edge anchor)
- .modal--notify or #notify-when-available-modal (the modal container)
- .btn--notify (if styled with transforms — likely not, but include for safety)

FILE: scripts/check-overlay-css.py

LOCATION: OVERLAY_PATTERNS dict/list near the top of the file. Match existing entry format.

OWNER: OC via /offload (<10 LoC delta in one file).

DEPENDS ON: T2 + T3 (snippets must exist before lint patterns reference their selectors).
BLOCKS: T5 (smoke test runs the lint; must pass).
design: Open scripts/check-overlay-css.py, find OVERLAY_PATTERNS (per CLAUDE.md doc says: "near the top of scripts/check-overlay-css.py"). Add the three new selectors using the existing entry format (regex pattern + a human-readable name). Run python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py to confirm the lint still exits 0 with the new patterns added.
acceptance_criteria:
